1	---
2	title: "OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation for cosmo-smoke"
3	created: "2026-04-18"
4	status: DRAFT
5	idea_ref: IDEA-MO1FBPJB
6	tags: [opentelemetry, tracing, observability, jaeger]
7	---
8	
9	# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation
10	
11	## Problem
12	
13	Smoke tests verify infrastructure health but are invisible to observability pipelines. When a smoke test fails, there's no trace in Jaeger/Tempo to correlate with. Teams can't answer: "did our smoke test traffic actually generate traces?" or "is the trace pipeline working end-to-end?"
14	
15	## Solution
16	
17	Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a dedicated `otel_trace` assertion that verifies traces arrive at a Jaeger-compatible collector.
18	
19	- Smoke generates its own trace context (no OTel SDK dependency)
20	- Traceparent headers auto-injected into all network assertions when `otel` config is enabled
21	- `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger API to confirm trace reception
22	- Zero overhead when disabled
23	
24	## Configuration Schema
25	
26	### Global `otel` block
27	
28	```yaml
29	otel:
30	  enabled: true
31	  collector_url: "http://jaeger:16686"
32	  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"      # default: "smoke"
33	  trace_propagation: true          # auto-inject traceparent into network assertions
34	```
35	
36	### Per-test `otel_trace` assertion
37	
38	```yaml
39	tests:
40	  - name: trace arrived at collector
41	    run: curl http://localhost:8080/health
42	    expect:
43	      otel_trace:
44	        collector_url: "http://jaeger:16686"   # overrides global
45	        service_name: "my-service"             # overrides global
46	        min_spans: 1                           # at least N spans received (default: 1)
47	        timeout: 5s                            # wait for trace to appear
48	```
49	
50	## Trace Context Model
51	
52	### W3C Traceparent Format
53	
54	All propagation uses the W3C `traceparent` header: `00-{traceID}-{spanID}-{flags}`
55	
56	- `traceID`: 32 hex chars (16 bytes), generated once per suite run
57	- `spanID`: 16 hex chars (8 bytes), generated per test
58	- `flags`: `01` (sampled)
59	
60	No OTel SDK dependency. Pure string construction from crypto/rand.
61	
62	### Hierarchy
63	
64	```
65	Suite (traceID)
66	├── Test 1 (spanID-1, traceparent injected into HTTP/gRPC/WS requests)
67	├── Test 2 (spanID-2, ...)
68	└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
69	```
70	
71	Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.
72	
73	### Injection Points
74	
75	| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
76	|---------------|-----------------|
77	| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
78	| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
79	| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |
80	
81	When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.
82	
83	## Trace Verification
84	
85	### Jaeger API Query
86	
87	The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:
88	
89	```
90	GET {collector_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name}
91	```
92	
93	### Assertion Logic
94	
95	1. Build the Jaeger API URL from `collector_url` + `traceID` + `service_name`
96	2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
97	3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
98	4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
99	5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail
100	
101	### Error Cases
102	
103	| Condition | Result |
104	|-----------|--------|
105	| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
106	| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
107	| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |
108	
109	### Jaeger Response Parsing
110	
111	Expected JSON structure:
112	
113	```json
114	{
115	  "data": [
116	    {
117	      "traceID": "abc123...",
118	      "spans": [
119	        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
120	      ]
121	    }
122	  ]
123	}
124	```
125	
126	Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.
127	
128	## Architecture
129	
130	### New Files
131	
132	| File | Purpose |
133	|------|---------|
134	| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
135	| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
136	| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
137	| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |
138	
139	### Modified Files
140	
141	| File | Change |
142	|------|--------|
143	| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
144	| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: collector_url format, service_name required when enabled |
145	| `internal/runner/runner.go` | Initialize `traceContext` in `Run()`, pass to assertion execution |
146	| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
147	| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
148	| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
149	| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag as shortcut to override `otel.collector_url` |
150	
151	### Data Flow
152	
153	```
154	smoke run
155	  └── Runner.Run(opts)
156	       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
157	       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
158	       ├── For each test:
159	       │    ├── Create child spanID
160	       │    ├── Run test command
161	       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
162	       │    │    └── Inject traceparent: 00-{traceID}-{spanID}-01
163	       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
164	       │    │    └── Poll GET {collector}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}
165	       │    └── Collect AssertionResult
166	       └── Return SuiteResult
167	```
168	
169	### No New Dependencies
170	
171	All stdlib: `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`, `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `fmt`. W3C traceparent is a simple string format.
172	
173	## Design Decisions
174	
175	| Decision | Rationale |
176	|----------|-----------|
177	| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |
178	| Jaeger API only (v1) | Most widely deployed. Tempo is Jaeger-compatible. Can add backends later. |
179	| Suite-level traceID | Correlates all tests from one `smoke run` invocation. Matches how teams use traces. |
180	| Per-test spanID | Allows identifying which test generated which downstream span. |
181	| Polling for verification | Traces take time to propagate through collector pipeline. 500ms interval balances latency vs load. |
182	| `min_spans` threshold | Default 1 means "any trace arrived." Configurable for stricter checks. |
183	| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `collector_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |
184	
185	## Edge Cases
186	
187	- **No `otel` config**: No trace context created, no injection, no overhead
188	- **`otel` enabled but no network assertions**: Trace context created but unused. `otel_trace` assertion still works standalone.
189	- **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
190	- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
191	- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts
192	
193	## Future Considerations
194	
195	- Support additional backends (Tempo native API, Honeycomb, Datadog)
196	- Export smoke results as OTel spans (reverse direction)
197	- Trace-aware retries: only retry if trace was never received
198	- Integration with `--watch` mode: continuous trace health monitoring
199

---
title: "OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation for cosmo-smoke"
created: "2026-04-18"
status: DRAFT
idea_ref: IDEA-MO1FBPJB
tags: [opentelemetry, tracing, observability, jaeger]
---

# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation

## Problem

Smoke tests verify infrastructure health but are invisible to observability pipelines. When a smoke test fails, there's no trace in Jaeger/Tempo to correlate with. Teams can't answer: "did our smoke test traffic actually generate traces?" or "is the trace pipeline working end-to-end?"

## Solution

Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a dedicated `otel_trace` assertion that verifies traces arrive at a Jaeger-compatible collector.

- Smoke generates its own trace context (no OTel SDK dependency)
- Traceparent headers auto-injected into all network assertions when `otel` config is enabled
- `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger API to confirm trace reception
- Zero overhead when disabled

## Configuration Schema

### Global `otel` block

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  collector_url: "http://jaeger:16686"
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"      # default: "smoke"
  trace_propagation: true          # auto-inject traceparent into network assertions
```

### Per-test `otel_trace` assertion

```yaml
tests:
  - name: trace arrived at collector
    run: curl http://localhost:8080/health
    expect:
      otel_trace:
        collector_url: "http://jaeger:16686"   # overrides global
        service_name: "my-service"             # overrides global
        min_spans: 1                           # at least N spans received (default: 1)
        timeout: 5s                            # wait for trace to appear
```

## Trace Context Model

### W3C Traceparent Format

All propagation uses the W3C `traceparent` header: `00-{traceID}-{spanID}-{flags}`

- `traceID`: 32 hex chars (16 bytes), generated once per suite run
- `spanID`: 16 hex chars (8 bytes), generated per test
- `flags`: `01` (sampled)

No OTel SDK dependency. Pure string construction from crypto/rand.

### Hierarchy

```
Suite (traceID)
├── Test 1 (spanID-1, traceparent injected into HTTP/gRPC/WS requests)
├── Test 2 (spanID-2, ...)
└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
```

Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.

### Injection Points

| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
|---------------|-----------------|
| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |

When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.

## Trace Verification

### Jaeger API Query

The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:

```
GET {collector_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name}
```

### Assertion Logic

1. Build the Jaeger API URL from `collector_url` + `traceID` + `service_name`
2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail

### Error Cases

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |

### Jaeger Response Parsing

Expected JSON structure:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "traceID": "abc123...",
      "spans": [
        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.

## Architecture

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: collector_url format, service_name required when enabled |
| `internal/runner/runner.go` | Initialize `traceContext` in `Run()`, pass to assertion execution |
| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag as shortcut to override `otel.collector_url` |

### Data Flow

```
smoke run
  └── Runner.Run(opts)
       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
       ├── For each test:
       │    ├── Create child spanID
       │    ├── Run test command
       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
       │    │    └── Inject traceparent: 00-{traceID}-{spanID}-01
       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
       │    │    └── Poll GET {collector}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}
       │    └── Collect AssertionResult
       └── Return SuiteResult
```

### No New Dependencies

All stdlib: `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`, `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `fmt`. W3C traceparent is a simple string format.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |
| Jaeger API only (v1) | Most widely deployed. Tempo is Jaeger-compatible. Can add backends later. |

# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation

## Problem

Smoke tests verify infrastructure health but are invisible to observability pipelines. When a smoke test fails, there's no trace in Jaeger/Tempo to correlate with. Teams can't answer: "did our smoke test traffic actually generate traces?" or "is the trace pipeline working end-to-end?"

## Solution

Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a dedicated `otel_trace` assertion that verifies traces arrive at a Jaeger-compatible collector.

- Smoke generates its own trace context (no OTel SDK dependency)
- Traceparent headers auto-injected into all network assertions when `otel` config is enabled
- `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger API to confirm trace reception
- Zero overhead when disabled

## Configuration Schema

### Global `otel` block

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  collector_url: "http://jaeger:16686"
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"      # default: "smoke"
  trace_propagation: true          # auto-inject traceparent into network assertions
```

### Per-test `otel_trace` assertion

```yaml
tests:
  - name: trace arrived at collector
    run: curl http://localhost:8080/health
    expect:
      otel_trace:
        collector_url: "http://jaeger:16686"   # overrides global
        service_name: "my-service"             # overrides global
        min_spans: 1                           # at least N spans received (default: 1)
        timeout: 5s                            # wait for trace to appear
```

## Trace Context Model

### W3C Traceparent Format

All propagation uses the W3C `traceparent` header: `00-{traceID}-{spanID}-{flags}`

- `traceID`: 32 hex chars (16 bytes), generated once per suite run
- `spanID`: 16 hex chars (8 bytes), generated per test
- `flags`: `01` (sampled)

No OTel SDK dependency. Pure string construction from crypto/rand.

### Hierarchy

```
Suite (traceID)
├── Test 1 (spanID-1, traceparent injected into HTTP/gRPC/WS requests)
├── Test 2 (spanID-2, ...)
└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
```

Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.

### Injection Points

| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
|---------------|-----------------|
| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |

When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.

## Trace Verification

### Jaeger API Query

The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:

```
GET {collector_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name}
```

### Assertion Logic

1. Build the Jaeger API URL from `collector_url` + `traceID` + `service_name`
2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail

### Error Cases

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |

### Jaeger Response Parsing

Expected JSON structure:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "traceID": "abc123...",
      "spans": [
        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.

## Architecture

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: collector_url format, service_name required when enabled |
| `internal/runner/runner.go` | Initialize `traceContext` in `Run()`, pass to assertion execution |
| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag as shortcut to override `otel.collector_url` |

### Data Flow

```
smoke run
  └── Runner.Run(opts)
       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
       ├── For each test:
       │    ├── Create child spanID
       │    ├── Run test command
       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
       │    │    └── Inject traceparent: 00-{traceID}-{spanID}-01
       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
       │    │    └── Poll GET {collector}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}
       │    └── Collect AssertionResult
       └── Return SuiteResult
```

### No New Dependencies

All stdlib: `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`, `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `fmt`. W3C traceparent is a simple string format.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |
| Jaeger API only (v1) | Most widely deployed. Tempo is Jaeger-compatible. Can add backends later. |
| Suite-level traceID | Correlates all tests from one `smoke run` invocation. Matches how teams use traces. |
| Per-test spanID | Allows identifying which test generated which downstream span. |
| Polling for verification | Traces take time to propagate through collector pipeline. 500ms interval balances latency vs load. |
| `min_spans` threshold | Default 1 means "any trace arrived." Configurable for stricter checks. |
| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `collector_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |

## Edge Cases

- **No `otel` config**: No trace context created, no injection, no overhead
- **`otel` enabled but no network assertions**: Trace context created but unused. `otel_trace` assertion still works standalone.
- **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts

## Future Considerations

- Support additional backends (Tempo native API, Honeycomb, Datadog)
- Export smoke results as OTel spans (reverse direction)
- Trace-aware retries: only retry if trace was never received
- Integration with `--watch` mode: continuous trace health monitoring

| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |

| Polling for verification | Traces take time to propagate through collector pipeline. 500ms interval balances latency vs load. |
| `min_spans` threshold | Default 1 means "any trace arrived." Configurable for stricter checks. |
| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `collector_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |

## Edge Cases

- **No `otel` config**: No trace context created, no injection, no overhead
- **`otel` enabled but no network assertions**: Trace context created but unused. `otel_trace` assertion still works standalone.
- **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts

## Future Considerations

- Support additional backends (Tempo native API, Honeycomb, Datadog)
- Export smoke results as OTel spans (reverse direction)
- Trace-aware retries: only retry if trace was never received
- Integration with `--watch` mode: continuous trace health monitoring

title: "OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation for cosmo-smoke"
created: "2026-04-18"
status: DRAFT
idea_ref: IDEA-MO1FBPJB
tags: [opentelemetry, tracing, observability, jaeger]
---

# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation

## Problem

Smoke tests verify infrastructure health but are invisible to observability pipelines. When a smoke test fails, there's no trace in Jaeger/Tempo to correlate with. Teams can't answer: "did our smoke test traffic actually generate traces?" or "is the trace pipeline working end-to-end?"

## Solution

Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a dedicated `otel_trace` assertion that verifies traces arrive at a Jaeger-compatible collector.

- Smoke generates its own trace context (no OTel SDK dependency)
- Traceparent headers auto-injected into all network assertions when `otel` config is enabled
- `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger API to confirm trace reception
- Zero overhead when disabled

## Configuration Schema

### Global `otel` block

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"   # Jaeger query API (not OTLP ingest)
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"         # default: "smoke"
  trace_propagation: true             # auto-inject traceparent into network assertions
```

### Per-test `otel_trace` assertion

```yaml
tests:
  - name: trace arrived at collector
    run: curl http://localhost:8080/health
    expect:
      otel_trace:
        jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"      # overrides global
        service_name: "my-service"             # overrides global
        min_spans: 1                           # at least N spans received (default: 1)
        timeout: 5s                            # wait for trace to appear
```

## Trace Context Model

### W3C Traceparent Format

All propagation uses the W3C `traceparent` header: `00-{traceID}-{spanID}-{flags}`

- `traceID`: 32 hex chars (16 bytes), generated once per suite run
- `spanID`: 16 hex chars (8 bytes), generated per test
- `flags`: `01` (sampled)

No OTel SDK dependency. Pure string construction from crypto/rand. The `tracestate` header is intentionally omitted — cosmo-smoke doesn't need vendor-specific trace context.

### Hierarchy

```
Suite (traceID)
├── Test 1 (spanID-1, traceparent injected into HTTP/gRPC/WS requests)
├── Test 2 (spanID-2, ...)
└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
```

Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.

### Injection Points

| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
|---------------|-----------------|
| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |

When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.

## Trace Verification

### Jaeger API Query

The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:

```
GET {collector_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name}
```

### Assertion Logic

1. Build the Jaeger API URL from `collector_url` + `traceID` + `service_name`
2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail

### Error Cases

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |

### Jaeger Response Parsing

Expected JSON structure:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "traceID": "abc123...",
      "spans": [
        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.

## Architecture

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: collector_url format, service_name required when enabled |
| `internal/runner/runner.go` | Initialize `traceContext` in `Run()`, pass to assertion execution |
| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag as shortcut to override `otel.collector_url` |

### Data Flow

```
smoke run
  └── Runner.Run(opts)
       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
       ├── For each test:
       │    ├── Create child spanID
       │    ├── Run test command
       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
       │    │    └── Inject traceparent: 00-{traceID}-{spanID}-01
       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
       │    │    └── Poll GET {collector}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}
       │    └── Collect AssertionResult
       └── Return SuiteResult
```

### No New Dependencies

All stdlib: `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`, `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `fmt`. W3C traceparent is a simple string format.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |
| Jaeger API only (v1) | Most widely deployed. Tempo is Jaeger-compatible. Can add backends later. |
| Suite-level traceID | Correlates all tests from one `smoke run` invocation. Matches how teams use traces. |
| Per-test spanID | Allows identifying which test generated which downstream span. |
| Polling for verification | Traces take time to propagate through collector pipeline. 500ms interval balances latency vs load. |
| `min_spans` threshold | Default 1 means "any trace arrived." Configurable for stricter checks. |
| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `collector_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |

## Edge Cases

- **No `otel` config**: No trace context created, no injection, no overhead
- **`otel` enabled but no network assertions**: Trace context created but unused. `otel_trace` assertion still works standalone.
- **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts

## Future Considerations

- Support additional backends (Tempo native API, Honeycomb, Datadog)
- Export smoke results as OTel spans (reverse direction)
- Trace-aware retries: only retry if trace was never received
- Integration with `--watch` mode: continuous trace health monitoring

### Data Flow

```

  └── Runner.Run(opts)
       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
       ├── For each test:
       │    ├── Create child spanID
       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
```
```
       │    │    └── Inject traceparent into request BEFORE sending
       │    │        (at request-construction time inside each Check* function)
       │    ├── Run test command
       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
       │    │    └── Poll GET {jaeger}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}

```

All 26 existing assertions are pure functions — no I/O beyond test command output. `otel_trace` departs from this by polling an external HTTP API. This is intentional: trace verification is inherently an external system check (like `port_listening` or `redis_ping`). The function signature accepts an `*http.Client` parameter for testability — tests inject an `httptest` mock, production uses the default client.

```
└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
```

Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.

### Injection Points

| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
|---------------|-----------------|
| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |

When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.

## Trace Verification

### Jaeger API Query

The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:

```
GET {jaeger_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name}
```

### Assertion Logic

1. Build the Jaeger API URL from `jaeger_url` + `traceID` + `service_name`
2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail

### Error Cases

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |

### Jaeger Response Parsing

Expected JSON structure:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "traceID": "abc123...",
      "spans": [
        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.

## Architecture

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: collector_url format, service_name required when enabled |
| `internal/runner/runner.go` | Initialize `traceContext` in `Run()`, pass to assertion execution |
| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag as shortcut to override `otel.collector_url` |

### Data Flow

```
smoke run
  └── Runner.Run(opts)
       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
       ├── For each test:
       │    ├── Create child spanID
       │    ├── Run test command
       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
       │    │    └── Inject traceparent: 00-{traceID}-{spanID}-01
       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
       │    │    └── Poll GET {collector}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}
       │    └── Collect AssertionResult
       └── Return SuiteResult
```

### No New Dependencies

All stdlib: `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`, `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `fmt`. W3C traceparent is a simple string format.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |
| Jaeger API only (v1) | Most widely deployed. Tempo is Jaeger-compatible. Can add backends later. |
| Suite-level traceID | Correlates all tests from one `smoke run` invocation. Matches how teams use traces. |
| Per-test spanID | Allows identifying which test generated which downstream span. |
| Polling for verification | Traces take time to propagate through collector pipeline. 500ms interval balances latency vs load. |
| `min_spans` threshold | Default 1 means "any trace arrived." Configurable for stricter checks. |
| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `collector_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |

## Edge Cases

- **No `otel` config**: No trace context created, no injection, no overhead
- **`otel` enabled but no network assertions**: Trace context created but unused. `otel_trace` assertion still works standalone.
- **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts

## Future Considerations

- Support additional backends (Tempo native API, Honeycomb, Datadog)
- Export smoke results as OTel spans (reverse direction)
- Trace-aware retries: only retry if trace was never received
- Integration with `--watch` mode: continuous trace health monitoring

# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation

## Problem

Smoke tests verify infrastructure health but are invisible to observability pipelines. When a smoke test fails, there's no trace in Jaeger/Tempo to correlate with. Teams can't answer: "did our smoke test traffic actually generate traces?" or "is the trace pipeline working end-to-end?"

## Solution

Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a dedicated `otel_trace` assertion that verifies traces arrive at a Jaeger-compatible collector.

- Smoke generates its own trace context (no OTel SDK dependency)
- Traceparent headers auto-injected into all network assertions when `otel` config is enabled
- `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger API to confirm trace reception
- Zero overhead when disabled

## Configuration Schema

### Global `otel` block

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"   # Jaeger query API (not OTLP ingest)
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"         # default: "smoke"
  trace_propagation: true             # auto-inject traceparent into network assertions
```

### Per-test `otel_trace` assertion

```yaml
tests:
  - name: trace arrived at collector
    run: curl http://localhost:8080/health
    expect:
      otel_trace:
        jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"      # overrides global
        service_name: "my-service"             # overrides global
        min_spans: 1                           # at least N spans received (default: 1)
        timeout: 5s                            # wait for trace to appear
```

## Trace Context Model

### W3C Traceparent Format

All propagation uses the W3C `traceparent` header: `00-{traceID}-{spanID}-{flags}`

- `traceID`: 32 hex chars (16 bytes), generated once per suite run
- `spanID`: 16 hex chars (8 bytes), generated per test
- `flags`: `01` (sampled)

No OTel SDK dependency. Pure string construction from crypto/rand. The `tracestate` header is intentionally omitted — cosmo-smoke doesn't need vendor-specific trace context.

### Hierarchy

```
Suite (traceID)
├── Test 1 (spanID-1, traceparent injected into HTTP/gRPC/WS requests)
├── Test 2 (spanID-2, ...)
└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
```

Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.

| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
|---------------|-----------------|
| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |

When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.

### Jaeger API Query

The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:

```

```

### Assertion Logic

2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail

### Error Cases

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |

### Jaeger Response Parsing

Expected JSON structure:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "traceID": "abc123...",
      "spans": [
        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.

## Architecture

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: collector_url format, service_name required when enabled |

| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag as shortcut to override `otel.collector_url` |

### Data Flow

```
smoke run
  └── Runner.Run(opts)
       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
```

| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `collector_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |

- **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts

- Support additional backends (Tempo native API, Honeycomb, Datadog)
- Export smoke results as OTel spans (reverse direction)
- Trace-aware retries: only retry if trace was never received

---
title: "OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation for cosmo-smoke"
created: "2026-04-18"
status: DRAFT
idea_ref: IDEA-MO1FBPJB
tags: [opentelemetry, tracing, observability, jaeger]
---

# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation

## Problem

Smoke tests verify infrastructure health but are invisible to observability pipelines. When a smoke test fails, there's no trace in Jaeger/Tempo to correlate with. Teams can't answer: "did our smoke test traffic actually generate traces?" or "is the trace pipeline working end-to-end?"

## Solution

Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a dedicated `otel_trace` assertion that verifies traces arrive at a Jaeger-compatible collector.

- Smoke generates its own trace context (no OTel SDK dependency)
- Traceparent headers auto-injected into all network assertions when `otel` config is enabled
- `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger API to confirm trace reception
- Zero overhead when disabled

## Configuration Schema

### Global `otel` block

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"   # Jaeger query API (not OTLP ingest)
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"         # default: "smoke"
  trace_propagation: true             # auto-inject traceparent into network assertions
```

### Per-test `otel_trace` assertion

```yaml
tests:
  - name: trace arrived at collector
    run: curl http://localhost:8080/health
    expect:
      otel_trace:
        jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"      # overrides global
        service_name: "my-service"             # overrides global
        min_spans: 1                           # at least N spans received (default: 1)
        timeout: 5s                            # wait for trace to appear
```

## Trace Context Model

### W3C Traceparent Format

All propagation uses the W3C `traceparent` header: `00-{traceID}-{spanID}-{flags}`

- `traceID`: 32 hex chars (16 bytes), generated once per suite run
- `spanID`: 16 hex chars (8 bytes), generated per test
- `flags`: `01` (sampled)

No OTel SDK dependency. Pure string construction from crypto/rand. The `tracestate` header is intentionally omitted — cosmo-smoke doesn't need vendor-specific trace context.

### Hierarchy

```
Suite (traceID)
├── Test 1 (spanID-1, traceparent injected into HTTP/gRPC/WS requests)
├── Test 2 (spanID-2, ...)
└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
```

Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.

### Injection Points

| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
|---------------|-----------------|
| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |

When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.

## Trace Verification

### Jaeger API Query

The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:

```
GET {jaeger_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name}
```

### Assertion Logic

1. Build the Jaeger API URL from `jaeger_url` + `traceID` + `service_name`
2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail

### Error Cases

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |

### Jaeger Response Parsing

Expected JSON structure:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "traceID": "abc123...",
      "spans": [
        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.

## Architecture

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: jaeger_url format, service_name required when enabled |
| `internal/runner/runner.go` | Initialize `traceContext` in `Run()`, pass to assertion execution |
| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag (override `otel.jaeger_url`) and `--no-otel` flag (disable at runtime) |

### Data Flow

```
smoke run
  └── Runner.Run(opts)
       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
       ├── For each test:
       │    ├── Create child spanID
       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
       │    │    └── Inject traceparent into request BEFORE sending
       │    │        (at request-construction time inside each Check* function)
       │    ├── Run test command
       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
       │    │    └── Poll GET {jaeger}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}
       │    └── Collect AssertionResult
       └── Return SuiteResult
```

### Impure Assertion Note

All 26 existing assertions are pure functions — no I/O beyond test command output. `otel_trace` departs from this by polling an external HTTP API. This is intentional: trace verification is inherently an external system check (like `port_listening` or `redis_ping`). The function signature accepts an `*http.Client` parameter for testability — tests inject an `httptest` mock, production uses the default client.

### No New Dependencies

All stdlib: `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`, `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `fmt`. W3C traceparent is a simple string format.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |
| Jaeger API only (v1) | Most widely deployed. Tempo is Jaeger-compatible. Can add backends later. |
| Suite-level traceID | Correlates all tests from one `smoke run` invocation. Matches how teams use traces. |
| Per-test spanID | Allows identifying which test generated which downstream span. |
| Polling for verification | Traces take time to propagate through collector pipeline. 500ms interval balances latency vs load. |
| `min_spans` threshold | Default 1 means "any trace arrived." Configurable for stricter checks. |
| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `collector_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |

## Edge Cases

- **No `otel` config**: No trace context created, no injection, no overhead
- **`otel` enabled but no network assertions**: Trace context created but unused. `otel_trace` assertion still works standalone.
- **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts

## Future Considerations

- Support additional backends (Tempo native API, Honeycomb, Datadog)
- Export smoke results as OTel spans (reverse direction)
- Trace-aware retries: only retry if trace was never received
- Integration with `--watch` mode: continuous trace health monitoring

lines: - **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts

+- **`allow_failure` interaction**: Trace context creation and injection are independent of assertion pass/fail. A test with `allow_failure: true` still gets a spanID and traceparent injection — the `otel_trace` assertion may fail without affecting suite outcome.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/gab/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.0.2/skills/writing-plans

# Writing Plans

## Overview

Write comprehensive implementation plans assuming the engineer has zero context for our codebase and questionable taste. Document everything they need to know: which files to touch for each task, code, testing, docs they might need to check, how to test it. Give them the whole plan as bite-sized tasks. DRY. YAGNI. TDD. Frequent commits.

Assume they are a skilled developer, but know almost nothing about our toolset or problem domain. Assume they don't know good test design very well.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the writing-plans skill to create the implementation plan."

**Context:** This should be run in a dedicated worktree (created by brainstorming skill).

**Save plans to:** `docs/superpowers/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md`
- (User preferences for plan location override this default)

## Scope Check

If the spec covers multiple independent subsystems, it should have been broken into sub-project specs during brainstorming. If it wasn't, suggest breaking this into separate plans — one per subsystem. Each plan should produce working, testable software on its own.

## File Structure

Before defining tasks, map out which files will be created or modified and what each one is responsible for. This is where decomposition decisions get locked in.

- Design units with clear boundaries and well-defined interfaces. Each file should have one clear responsibility.
- You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. Prefer smaller, focused files over large ones that do too much.
- Files that change together should live together. Split by responsibility, not by technical layer.
- In existing codebases, follow established patterns. If the codebase uses large files, don't unilaterally restructure - but if a file you're modifying has grown unwieldy, including a split in the plan is reasonable.

This structure informs the task decomposition. Each task should produce self-contained changes that make sense independently.

## Bite-Sized Task Granularity

**Each step is one action (2-5 minutes):**
- "Write the failing test" - step
- "Run it to make sure it fails" - step
- "Implement the minimal code to make the test pass" - step
- "Run the tests and make sure they pass" - step
- "Commit" - step

## Plan Document Header

**Every plan MUST start with this header:**

```markdown
# [Feature Name] Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** [One sentence describing what this builds]

**Architecture:** [2-3 sentences about approach]

**Tech Stack:** [Key technologies/libraries]

---
```

## Task Structure

````markdown
### Task N: [Component Name]

**Files:**
- Create: `exact/path/to/file.py`
- Modify: `exact/path/to/existing.py:123-145`
- Test: `tests/exact/path/to/test.py`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```python
def test_specific_behavior():
    result = function(input)
    assert result == expected
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: FAIL with "function not defined"

- [ ] **Step 3: Write minimal implementation**

```python
def function(input):
    return expected
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `pytest tests/path/test.py::test_name -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add tests/path/test.py src/path/file.py
git commit -m "feat: add specific feature"
```
````

## Remember
- Exact file paths always
- Complete code in plan (not "add validation")
- Exact commands with expected output
- Reference relevant skills with @ syntax
- DRY, YAGNI, TDD, frequent commits

## Plan Review Loop

After completing each chunk of the plan:

1. Dispatch plan-document-reviewer subagent (see plan-document-reviewer-prompt.md) with precisely crafted review context — never your session history. This keeps the reviewer focused on the plan, not your thought process.
   - Provide: chunk content, path to spec document
2. If ❌ Issues Found:
   - Fix the issues in the chunk
   - Re-dispatch reviewer for that chunk
   - Repeat until ✅ Approved
3. If ✅ Approved: proceed to next chunk (or execution handoff if last chunk)

**Chunk boundaries:** Use `## Chunk N: <name>` headings to delimit chunks. Each chunk should be ≤1000 lines and logically self-contained.

**Review loop guidance:**
- Same agent that wrote the plan fixes it (preserves context)
- If loop exceeds 5 iterations, surface to human for guidance
- Reviewers are advisory - explain disagreements if you believe feedback is incorrect

## Execution Handoff

After saving the plan:

**"Plan complete and saved to `docs/superpowers/plans/<filename>.md`. Ready to execute?"**

**Execution path depends on harness capabilities:**

**If harness has subagents (Claude Code, etc.):**
- **REQUIRED:** Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development
- Do NOT offer a choice - subagent-driven is the standard approach
- Fresh subagent per task + two-stage review

**If harness does NOT have subagents:**
- Execute plan in current session using superpowers:executing-plans
- Batch execution with checkpoints for review

input.prompt:
Review the implementation plan at `/Users/gab/PROJECTS/cosmo-smoke/docs/planning-mode/2026-04-19-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md` against the spec at `/Users/gab/PROJECTS/cosmo-smoke/docs/brainstorming/2026-04-18-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md`.

Check:
1. **Completeness**: Does the plan cover every file change listed in the spec's architecture section?
2. **TDD compliance**: Does every task have write-test-first → verify-fail → implement → verify-pass steps?
3. **Task independence**: Can each task be executed independently? Are dependencies clear?
4. **Exact code**: Does the plan include complete code (not "add validation") for each step?
5. **File paths**: Are all file paths correct for the cosmo-smoke project?
6. **Missing steps**: Anything the spec requires but the plan doesn't cover?

Return APPROVED or ISSUES FOUND with details. Under 200 words.

content.text:
I have all the data I need. Here is my review.

---

**ISSUES FOUND**

**1. Line number inaccuracies.** The plan says "SmokeConfig struct, around line 15-23" -- that is correct. It says "Expect struct, around line 99" -- the Expect struct ends at line 100, so that is close enough. But the plan says "validate.go:86-98" for the WebSocket validation block, which is accurate, and "validate.go:108-125" for `hasStandaloneAssertions`, which is accurate. No blocking issue here.

**2. Incomplete TDD on Task 9 (CLI flags).** Task 9 has no test at all -- it says "test manually" with a `--help` grep. This violates TDD compliance. The plan should include a programmatic test that verifies the flags override config correctly.

**3. Incomplete TDD on Task 10 (docs).** Task 10 is docs-only, so no test is expected. No issue here.

**4. Missing `WebSocketCheck.Headers` field handling.** Task 6 Step 3 says "add `Headers` field to `WebSocketCheck` in schema if not already present." The spec does not list this as a schema change -- it says injection is into "WebSocket handshake headers." The plan needs to verify whether `WebSocketCheck` already has a `Headers` field. If not, this is an undocumented schema modification that should be explicit in the File Structure table and validated in Task 2.

**5. Missing `GRPCHealthCheck.Metadata` field in schema changes.** Task 7 adds a `Metadata map[string]string` field to `GRPCHealthCheck`, but this field is not listed in the plan's File Structure table under modified files. It is also not covered by Task 1 (schema struct changes) or Task 2 (validation). This is a gap.

**6. Task dependency ordering issue.** Chunk 5 (Task 8, runner integration) depends on Tasks 5-7 (HTTP/WS/gRPC injection) but the plan does not state this dependency explicitly. An agent executing Task 8 before Tasks 5-7 would get compile errors on undefined `CheckHTTPWithTrace` etc.

**7. Spec requires `trace_propagation` conditional injection, plan implements it correctly.** The plan checks `r.Config.OTel.TracePropagation` before calling the `WithTrace` variants. This matches the spec. No issue.

**8. Missing assertion count update.** CLAUDE.md says "26 assertion types." Adding `otel_trace` makes 27. Task 10 should update that count.

**Verdict: ISSUES FOUND.** Three substantive gaps: (a) Task 9 has no automated test, (b) schema additions for `WebSocketCheck.Headers` and `GRPCHealthCheck.Metadata` are not tracked in the File Structure table or covered by Tasks 1/2, and (c) task dependencies between Chunk 4 and Chunk 5 are not explicit. These should be fixed before the plan is handed to an agent.

content.text: Now fix Task 9 (CLI flags) to add an automated test:

input.old_string:
### Task 9: Add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags

**Files:**
- Modify: `cmd/run.go`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add flags to run command**

In `cmd/run.go`, add persistent flags:

```go
runCmd.Flags().String("otel-collector", "", "Override otel.jaeger_url at runtime")
runCmd.Flags().Bool("no-otel", false, "Disable otel trace propagation for this run")
```

Wire in the `run` function:

```go
if v, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-otel"); v {
	cfg.OTel.Enabled = false
}
if v, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("otel-collector"); v != "" {
	cfg.OTel.JaegerURL = v
	cfg.OTel.Enabled = true
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Test manually**

Run: `go build -o smoke . && ./smoke run --help | grep otel`
Expected: Both flags visible in help output

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```
feat(cmd): add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags

- --otel-collector overrides jaeger_url and enables otel
- --no-otel disables trace propagation for this run

Refs: IDEA-MO1FBPJB
```
input.new_string:
### Task 9: Add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags

**Files:**
- Modify: `cmd/run.go`
- Test: `cmd/run_test.go` (or relevant test file)

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

```go
func TestRunFlags_OTelCollector(t *testing.T) {
	// Test that --otel-collector overrides config
	// Verify cfg.OTel.JaegerURL is set and cfg.OTel.Enabled is true
}

func TestRunFlags_NoOtel(t *testing.T) {
	// Test that --no-otel disables otel regardless of config
	// Verify cfg.OTel.Enabled is false
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

Run: `go test ./cmd/ -run "TestRunFlags_OTel|TestRunFlags_NoOtel" -v`
Expected: FAIL

- [ ] **Step 3: Add flags to run command**

In `cmd/run.go`, add flags:

```go
runCmd.Flags().String("otel-collector", "", "Override otel.jaeger_url at runtime")
runCmd.Flags().Bool("no-otel", false, "Disable otel trace propagation for this run")
```

Wire in the `run` function:

```go
if v, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-otel"); v {
	cfg.OTel.Enabled = false
}
if v, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("otel-collector"); v != "" {
	cfg.OTel.JaegerURL = v
	cfg.OTel.Enabled = true
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run tests**

Run: `go test ./cmd/ -run "TestRunFlags_OTel|TestRunFlags_NoOtel" -v`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```
feat(cmd): add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags

- --otel-collector overrides jaeger_url and enables otel
- --no-otel disables trace propagation for this run
- 2 tests covering flag override behavior

Refs: IDEA-MO1FBPJB
```

title: "OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation for cosmo-smoke"
created: "2026-04-18"
status: DRAFT
idea_ref: IDEA-MO1FBPJB
tags: [opentelemetry, tracing, observability, jaeger]
---

# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation

## Problem

Smoke tests verify infrastructure health but are invisible to observability pipelines. When a smoke test fails, there's no trace in Jaeger/Tempo to correlate with. Teams can't answer: "did our smoke test traffic actually generate traces?" or "is the trace pipeline working end-to-end?"

## Solution

Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a dedicated `otel_trace` assertion that verifies traces arrive at a Jaeger-compatible collector.

- Smoke generates its own trace context (no OTel SDK dependency)
- Traceparent headers auto-injected into all network assertions when `otel` config is enabled
- `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger API to confirm trace reception
- Zero overhead when disabled

## Configuration Schema

### Global `otel` block

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"   # Jaeger query API (not OTLP ingest)
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"         # default: "smoke"
  trace_propagation: true             # auto-inject traceparent into network assertions
```

### Per-test `otel_trace` assertion

```yaml
tests:
  - name: trace arrived at collector
    run: curl http://localhost:8080/health
    expect:
      otel_trace:
        jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"      # overrides global
        service_name: "my-service"             # overrides global
        min_spans: 1                           # at least N spans received (default: 1)
        timeout: 5s                            # wait for trace to appear
```

## Trace Context Model

### W3C Traceparent Format

All propagation uses the W3C `traceparent` header: `00-{traceID}-{spanID}-{flags}`

- `traceID`: 32 hex chars (16 bytes), generated once per suite run
- `spanID`: 16 hex chars (8 bytes), generated per test
- `flags`: `01` (sampled)

No OTel SDK dependency. Pure string construction from crypto/rand. The `tracestate` header is intentionally omitted — cosmo-smoke doesn't need vendor-specific trace context.

### Hierarchy

```
Suite (traceID)
├── Test 1 (spanID-1, traceparent injected into HTTP/gRPC/WS requests)
├── Test 2 (spanID-2, ...)
└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
```

Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.

### Injection Points

| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
|---------------|-----------------|
| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |

When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.

## Trace Verification

### Jaeger API Query

The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:

```
GET {jaeger_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name}
```

### Assertion Logic

1. Build the Jaeger API URL from `jaeger_url` + `traceID` + `service_name`
2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail

### Error Cases

| Condition | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |

### Jaeger Response Parsing

Expected JSON structure:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "traceID": "abc123...",
      "spans": [
        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.

## Architecture

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |

### Modified Files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: jaeger_url format, service_name required when enabled |
| `internal/runner/runner.go` | Initialize `traceContext` in `Run()`, pass to assertion execution |
| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag (override `otel.jaeger_url`) and `--no-otel` flag (disable at runtime) |

### Data Flow

```
smoke run
  └── Runner.Run(opts)
       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
       ├── For each test:
       │    ├── Create child spanID
       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
       │    │    └── Inject traceparent into request BEFORE sending
       │    │        (at request-construction time inside each Check* function)
       │    ├── Run test command
       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
       │    │    └── Poll GET {jaeger}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}
       │    └── Collect AssertionResult
       └── Return SuiteResult
```

### Impure Assertion Note

All 26 existing assertions are pure functions — no I/O beyond test command output. `otel_trace` departs from this by polling an external HTTP API. This is intentional: trace verification is inherently an external system check (like `port_listening` or `redis_ping`). The function signature accepts an `*http.Client` parameter for testability — tests inject an `httptest` mock, production uses the default client.

### No New Dependencies

All stdlib: `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`, `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `fmt`. W3C traceparent is a simple string format.

## Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |
| Jaeger API only (v1) | Most widely deployed. Tempo is Jaeger-compatible. Can add backends later. |
| Suite-level traceID | Correlates all tests from one `smoke run` invocation. Matches how teams use traces. |
| Per-test spanID | Allows identifying which test generated which downstream span. |
| Polling for verification | Traces take time to propagate through collector pipeline. 500ms interval balances latency vs load. |
| `min_spans` threshold | Default 1 means "any trace arrived." Configurable for stricter checks. |
| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `jaeger_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |

## Edge Cases

- **No `otel` config**: No trace context created, no injection, no overhead
- **`otel` enabled but no network assertions**: Trace context created but unused. `otel_trace` assertion still works standalone.
- **Multiple `otel_trace` assertions**: Each queries independently with its own timeout/collector config
- **Collector returns empty data**: Retries until timeout, then fails
- **W3C traceparent format compatibility**: Strict `00` version prefix, lowercase hex, 32+16+2 char parts
- **`allow_failure` interaction**: Trace context creation and injection are independent of assertion pass/fail. A test with `allow_failure: true` still gets a spanID and traceparent injection — the `otel_trace` assertion may fail without affecting suite outcome.

## Future Considerations

- Support additional backends (Tempo native API, Honeycomb, Datadog)
- Export smoke results as OTel spans (reverse direction)
- Trace-aware retries: only retry if trace was never received
- Integration with `--watch` mode: continuous trace health monitoring

# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation — Full Implementation

## Context

Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a Jaeger-based trace verification assertion. This makes smoke tests visible in observability pipelines and enables end-to-end trace pipeline validation.

Design spec: `docs/brainstorming/2026-04-18-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md`
Implementation plan: `docs/planning-mode/2026-04-19-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md`

## Goals

- [ ] Task 1: Add OTelConfig + OTelTraceCheck to schema
- [ ] Task 2: Add validation for otel config fields
- [ ] Task 3: Implement TraceContext + SpanContext
- [ ] Task 4: Implement CheckOTelTrace with Jaeger polling
- [ ] Task 5: Inject traceparent into HTTP requests
- [ ] Task 6: Inject traceparent into WebSocket handshake
- [ ] Task 7: Inject traceparent into gRPC metadata
- [ ] Task 8: Wire trace context into runner pipeline
- [ ] Task 9: Add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags
- [ ] Task 10: Update CLAUDE.md and self-smoke config

## Execution Strategy

Chunk 1 (Tasks 1-2) and Chunk 2 (Tasks 3-4) are independent — can run in parallel.
Chunk 3 (Tasks 5-7) depends on Chunks 1 and 2.
Chunk 4 (Task 8) depends on all prior chunks.
Chunk 5 (Tasks 9-10) depends on Chunk 4.

Recommended: GLM dispatch for independent chunks, Opus for runner integration.

        files: [cmd/run.go, CLAUDE.md]

# GLM Dispatch Manifest for: OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation
# Generated by /brainplan from: docs/planning-mode/2026-04-19-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md
# Usage: ccs glm-agent exec-batch <this-file>

brainstorm_ref: docs/brainstorming/2026-04-18-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md
plan_ref: docs/planning-mode/2026-04-19-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md
prompt_ref: docs/prompts/2026-04-19-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md

tasks:
  - task: |
      Add OpenTelemetry schema types to cosmo-smoke and their validation.

      In `internal/schema/schema.go`:

      1. Add OTelConfig struct after Settings:
      ```go
      type OTelConfig struct {
          Enabled          bool   `yaml:"enabled,omitempty"`
          JaegerURL        string `yaml:"jaeger_url,omitempty"`
          ServiceName      string `yaml:"service_name,omitempty"`
          TracePropagation bool   `yaml:"trace_propagation,omitempty"`
      }
      ```

      2. Add to SmokeConfig struct: `OTel OTelConfig \`yaml:"otel,omitempty"\``

      3. Add OTelTraceCheck struct:
      ```go
      type OTelTraceCheck struct {
          JaegerURL   string   `yaml:"jaeger_url,omitempty"`
          ServiceName string   `yaml:"service_name,omitempty"`
          MinSpans    int      `yaml:"min_spans,omitempty"`
          Timeout     Duration `yaml:"timeout,omitempty"`
      }
      ```

      4. Add to Expect struct: `OTelTrace *OTelTraceCheck \`yaml:"otel_trace,omitempty"\``

      5. Add `Headers map[string]string \`yaml:"headers,omitempty"\`` to WebSocketCheck
      6. Add `Metadata map[string]string \`yaml:"-"\`` to GRPCHealthCheck

      In `internal/schema/validate.go`:

      1. After WebSocket validation block, add otel_trace validation:
      ```go
      if e := t.Expect.OTelTrace; e != nil {
          if e.JaegerURL == "" && cfg.OTel.JaegerURL == "" {
              errs = append(errs, fmt.Sprintf("%s: otel_trace.jaeger_url is required", prefix))
          } else if e.JaegerURL != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(e.JaegerURL, "http://") && !strings.HasPrefix(e.JaegerURL, "https://") {
              errs = append(errs, fmt.Sprintf("%s: otel_trace.jaeger_url must start with http:// or https://", prefix))
          }
      }
      ```

      2. After test loop, add global otel validation:
      ```go
      if cfg.OTel.Enabled && cfg.OTel.JaegerURL == "" {
          errs = append(errs, "otel.jaeger_url is required when otel is enabled")
      }
      ```

      3. Add `e.OTelTrace != nil ||` to hasStandaloneAssertions

      Write tests in `internal/schema/schema_test.go` and `internal/schema/validate_test.go`:
      - TestSmokeConfig_OTel: parse YAML with otel block, verify all fields
      - TestValidate_OTelTraceRequiresJaegerURL: otel_trace without jaeger_url should fail
      - TestValidate_OTelEnabledRequiresJaegerURL: otel enabled without jaeger_url should fail

      Verify: `go test ./internal/schema/ -v`
      Commit: ccs commit-batch --message "feat(schema): add OTelConfig, OTelTraceCheck structs and validation"
    files:
      - internal/schema/schema.go
      - internal/schema/validate.go
      - internal/schema/schema_test.go
      - internal/schema/validate_test.go
    context_files:
      - internal/runner/assertion_ws.go
      - internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go
    issue: IDEA-MO1FBPJB
    max_turns: 25

  - task: |
      Create W3C trace context generation and Jaeger trace verification for cosmo-smoke.

      Create `internal/runner/trace.go`:
      ```go
      package runner

      import (
          "crypto/rand"
          "encoding/hex"
      )

      type TraceContext struct {
          traceID [16]byte
          Enabled bool
      }

      type SpanContext struct {
          traceID [16]byte
          spanID  [8]byte
      }

      func NewTraceContext() *TraceContext {
          var tc TraceContext
          rand.Read(tc.traceID[:])
          tc.Enabled = true
          return &tc
      }

      func (tc *TraceContext) TraceID() string {
          return hex.EncodeToString(tc.traceID[:])
      }

      func (tc *TraceContext) NewSpan() *SpanContext {
          var sc SpanContext
          sc.traceID = tc.traceID
          rand.Read(sc.spanID[:])
          return &sc
      }

      func (sc *SpanContext) SpanID() string {
          return hex.EncodeToString(sc.spanID[:])
      }

      func (sc *SpanContext) Traceparent() string {
          return "00-" + hex.EncodeToString(sc.traceID[:]) + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(sc.spanID[:]) + "-01"
      }
      ```

      Create `internal/runner/trace_test.go` with 6 tests:
      - TestNewTraceContext: verify 32-char hex traceID, Enabled=true
      - TestTraceContext_ChildSpan: verify 16-char hex spanID
      - TestSpanContext_Traceparent: verify format `00-{32hex}-{16hex}-01`
      - TestTraceContext_DifferentTraceIDs: two contexts have different IDs
      - TestTraceContext_DifferentSpanIDs: two spans have different IDs
      - TestDisabledTraceContext: zero-value TraceContext has Enabled=false

      Create `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` with CheckOTelTrace function:
      - Accepts: *schema.OTelTraceCheck, traceID string, *http.Client
      - Polls GET {jaeger_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name} at 500ms intervals
      - Returns AssertionResult with type "otel_trace"
      - Checks len(response.Data[0].Spans) >= minSpans
      - Default timeout: 5s, default minSpans: 1

      ```go
      package runner

      import (
          "crypto/rand"
          "encoding/hex"
      )

      type TraceContext struct {
          traceID [16]byte
          Enabled bool
      }

      type SpanContext struct {
          traceID [16]byte
          spanID  [8]byte
      }

      func NewTraceContext() *TraceContext {
          var tc TraceContext
          rand.Read(tc.traceID[:])
          tc.Enabled = true
          return &tc
      }

      func (tc *TraceContext) TraceID() string {
          return hex.EncodeToString(tc.traceID[:])
      }

      func (tc *TraceContext) NewSpan() *SpanContext {
          var sc SpanContext
          sc.traceID = tc.traceID
          rand.Read(sc.spanID[:])
          return &sc
      }

      func (sc *SpanContext) SpanID() string {
          return hex.EncodeToString(sc.spanID[:])
      }

      func (sc *SpanContext) Traceparent() string {
          return "00-" + hex.EncodeToString(sc.traceID[:]) + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(sc.spanID[:]) + "-01"
      }
      ```

content.text:
# Run Continuation - Resume Previous Work

Load a continuation prompt to pick up where a previous session left off.
Automatically detects worktree context and looks in the appropriate location.

## Usage

```bash
/run-continuation              # Show latest, confirm before loading
/run-continuation --yes        # Auto-load most recent (skip confirm)
/run-continuation --list       # Show all prompts, pick by number
/run-continuation --main       # Force main repo prompts (when in worktree)
/run-continuation api-feature  # Fuzzy match filename
```

## Execution

**This command delegates to `ccs prompts` for deterministic behavior.**

### 1. Get Prompts via CCS

| Flag | CCS Command | Result |
|------|-------------|--------|
| (default) | `ccs prompts --latest` | Get path to most recent prompt |
| `--list` | `ccs prompts --limit 10` | Show numbered list |
| `--main` | `ccs prompts --main --latest` | Force main repo |

CCS automatically:
- Detects worktree context (🌿 indicator)
- Checks `docs/prompts/worktrees/{name}/` for worktree-specific prompts
- Falls back to main `docs/prompts/` if no worktree prompts exist

### 2. Default Flow (no args)

1. Run `ccs prompts --latest` to get the most recent prompt path
2. Show the filename and ask with AskUserQuestion:
   - Header: "Load prompt"
   - Question: "Load this continuation prompt?"
   - Options: "Yes, load it" | "Show all prompts"

### 3. --yes Flag

Run `ccs prompts --latest`, read the file, display and follow it.

### 4. --list Flag

Run `ccs prompts --limit 10` to show recent prompts with status.
Use AskUserQuestion with numbered options for selection.

### 5. Fuzzy Match (filename arg)

Match the argument against output from `ccs prompts`.
If multiple matches, use AskUserQuestion to pick.

### 6. Load & Execute

Read the selected prompt file and display its full content:

```
✅ **Loading:** 2026-01-05-sop-system-continuation.md

---
[Full prompt content]
---
```

### 7. Auto-Create Tasks from Goals

After loading, parse the `## Goals` section and create a TaskCreate for each goal:
- Subject: goal title (from `### [ ] N. Title` lines)
- Description: first paragraph under each goal heading
- activeForm: present continuous of the goal title

Also create tasks for carry-over items if present.

Then follow the instructions in the prompt.

## Related

`/continuation-prompt` • `/handoff` • `ccs prompts --help`
