# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation — Full Implementation

## Context

Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a Jaeger-based trace verification assertion. This makes smoke tests visible in observability pipelines and enables end-to-end trace pipeline validation.

Design spec: `docs/brainstorming/2026-04-18-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md`
Implementation plan: `docs/planning-mode/2026-04-19-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md`

## Goals

- [ ] Task 1: Add OTelConfig + OTelTraceCheck to schema
- [ ] Task 2: Add validation for otel config fields
- [ ] Task 3: Implement TraceContext + SpanContext
- [ ] Task 4: Implement CheckOTelTrace with Jaeger polling
- [ ] Task 5: Inject traceparent into HTTP requests
- [ ] Task 6: Inject traceparent into WebSocket handshake
- [ ] Task 7: Inject traceparent into gRPC metadata
- [ ] Task 8: Wire trace context into runner pipeline
- [ ] Task 9: Add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags
- [ ] Task 10: Update CLAUDE.md and self-smoke config

## Execution Strategy

Chunk 1 (Tasks 1-2) and Chunk 2 (Tasks 3-4) are independent — can run in parallel.
Chunk 3 (Tasks 5-7) depends on Chunks 1 and 2.
Chunk 4 (Task 8) depends on all prior chunks.
Chunk 5 (Tasks 9-10) depends on Chunk 4.

Recommended: GLM dispatch for independent chunks, Opus for runner integration.

    agents:
      - task: "Schema + validation (Tasks 1-2)"
        model: sonnet
        files: [internal/schema/schema.go, internal/schema/validate.go, internal/schema/schema_test.go, internal/schema/validate_test.go]
        ready: true
      - task: "Trace context + otel assertion (Tasks 3-4)"
        model: sonnet
        files: [internal/runner/trace.go, internal/runner/trace_test.go, internal/runner/assertion_otel.go, internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go]
        ready: true
      - task: "Network assertion injection (Tasks 5-7)"
        model: sonnet
        files: [internal/runner/assertion_network.go, internal/runner/assertion_ws.go, internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go, internal/runner/assertion_grpc_stub.go]
        ready: false  # depends on schema + trace context
      - task: "Runner integration (Task 8)"
        model: opus
        files: [internal/runner/runner.go]
        ready: false  # depends on all above
      - task: "CLI + docs (Tasks 9-10)"
        model: sonnet
        files: [cmd/run.go, CLAUDE.md]
        ready: false  # depends on runner integration

- `internal/runner/trace.go`
- `internal/runner/trace_test.go`

func TestSmokeConfig_OTel(t *testing.T) {
	yaml := `
version: 1
project: test
otel:
  enabled: true
  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"
  service_name: "my-service"
  trace_propagation: true
tests:
  - name: otel check
    expect:
      otel_trace:
        jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"
        service_name: "my-service"
        min_spans: 1
        timeout: 5s
`
	cfg, err := LoadFromBytes([]byte(yaml))
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("parse error: %v", err)
	}
	if !cfg.OTel.Enabled {
		t.Error("expected otel.enabled = true")

	if cfg.OTel.JaegerURL != "http://jaeger:16686" {
		t.Errorf("jaeger_url = %q, want http://jaeger:16686", cfg.OTel.JaegerURL)
	}
	if cfg.OTel.ServiceName != "my-service" {
		t.Errorf("service_name = %q, want my-service", cfg.OTel.ServiceName)
	}
	if !cfg.OTel.TracePropagation {
		t.Error("expected trace_propagation = true")
	}

		t.Fatal("expected otel_trace assertion")
	}
	if cfg.Tests[0].Expect.OTelTrace.MinSpans != 1 {

}

14	// SmokeConfig is the top-level configuration parsed from .smoke.yaml.
15	type SmokeConfig struct {
16		Version     int            `yaml:"version"`
17		Project     string         `yaml:"project"`
18		Description string         `yaml:"description,omitempty"`
19		Includes    []string       `yaml:"includes,omitempty"`
20		Settings    Settings       `yaml:"settings,omitempty"`

24	
25	// Settings controls global test behavior.
26	type Settings struct {
27		Timeout         Duration `yaml:"timeout,omitempty"`
28		FailFast        bool     `yaml:"fail_fast,omitempty"`
29		Parallel        bool     `yaml:"parallel,omitempty"`
30		Monorepo        bool     `yaml:"monorepo,omitempty"`
31		MonorepoExclude []string `yaml:"monorepo_exclude,omitempty"`
32	}
33	
34	// Prerequisite is a command that must succeed before tests run.
35	type Prerequisite struct {
36		Name  string `yaml:"name"`
37		Check string `yaml:"check"`
38		Hint  string `yaml:"hint,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	// RetryPolicy configures automatic retry for flaky tests.
42	type RetryPolicy struct {
43		Count   int      `yaml:"count"`
44		Backoff Duration `yaml:"backoff"`
45	}
46	
47	// Test defines a single smoke test.
48	type Test struct {
49		Name         string       `yaml:"name"`

196		Bucket   string `yaml:"bucket"`

199	}

202	type VersionCheck struct {
203		Command string `yaml:"command"`
204		Pattern string `yaml:"pattern"`
205	}

208	type WebSocketCheck struct {
209		URL            string   `yaml:"url"`
210		Send           string   `yaml:"send,omitempty"`
211		ExpectContains string   `yaml:"expect_contains,omitempty"`
212		ExpectMatches  string   `yaml:"expect_matches,omitempty"`
213		Timeout        Duration `yaml:"timeout,omitempty"`
214	}
215	
216	// Duration wraps time.Duration for YAML unmarshaling from strings like "5s".
217	type Duration struct {
218		time.Duration
219	}
220	
221	func (d *Duration) UnmarshalYAML(value *yaml.Node) error {
222		var s string
223		if err := value.Decode(&s); err != nil {
224			return err
225		}
226		parsed, err := time.ParseDuration(s)
227		if err != nil {
228			return fmt.Errorf("invalid duration %q: %w", s, err)
229		}
230		d.Duration = parsed
231		return nil
232	}
233	
234	func (d Duration) MarshalYAML() (interface{}, error) {
235		return d.Duration.String(), nil
236	}
237	
238	// Load reads and parses a .smoke.yaml file from the given path.
239	// Supports Go templates ({{ .Env.FOO }}) and includes.
240	func Load(path string) (*SmokeConfig, error) {
241		return loadWithDepth(path, 0)
242	}
243	
244	func loadWithDepth(path string, depth int) (*SmokeConfig, error) {
245		if depth > 10 {
246			return nil, fmt.Errorf("include depth exceeded (max 10): circular includes?")
247		}
248	
249		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
250		if err != nil {
251			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading config: %w", err)
252		}
253	
254		// Process Go templates
255		processed, err := processTemplate(data)
256		if err != nil {
257			return nil, fmt.Errorf("processing template: %w", err)
258		}
259	
260		cfg, err := Parse(processed)
261		if err != nil {
262			return nil, err
263		}
264	
265		// Process includes
266		configDir := filepath.Dir(path)
267		for _, inc := range cfg.Includes {
268			incPath := inc
269			if !filepath.IsAbs(inc) {
270				incPath = filepath.Join(configDir, inc)
271			}
272	
273			incCfg, err := loadWithDepth(incPath, depth+1)
274			if err != nil {
275				return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading include %q: %w", inc, err)
276			}
277	
278			// Merge: included tests and prereqs are prepended
279			cfg.Prereqs = append(incCfg.Prereqs, cfg.Prereqs...)
280			cfg.Tests = append(incCfg.Tests, cfg.Tests...)
281		}
282	
283		return cfg, nil
284	}
285	
286	// processTemplate expands Go templates in the config.
287	// Available: .Env (environment variables map)
288	func processTemplate(data []byte) ([]byte, error) {
289		tmpl, err := template.New("config").Parse(string(data))
290		if err != nil {
291			return nil, err
292		}
293	
294		// Build template data
295		envMap := make(map[string]string)
296		for _, e := range os.Environ() {
297			if idx := bytes.IndexByte([]byte(e), '='); idx > 0 {
298				envMap[e[:idx]] = e[idx+1:]
299			}
300		}
301	
302		var buf bytes.Buffer
303		err = tmpl.Execute(&buf, map[string]any{
304			"Env": envMap,
305		})
306		if err != nil {
307			return nil, err
308		}
309	
310		return buf.Bytes(), nil
311	}
312	
313	// Parse parses raw YAML bytes into a SmokeConfig.
314	func Parse(data []byte) (*SmokeConfig, error) {
315		var cfg SmokeConfig
316		if err := yaml.Unmarshal(data, &cfg); err != nil {
317			return nil, fmt.Errorf("parsing config: %w", err)
318		}
319		return &cfg, nil
320	}
321	
322	// LoadDefault finds and loads .smoke.yaml from the current directory.
323	func LoadDefault() (*SmokeConfig, error) {
324		return Load(".smoke.yaml")
325	}
326	
327	// MergeEnv loads an environment-specific config and deep-merges it onto base.
328	// Env-specific tests are appended (not replaced). Settings from env override base.
329	func MergeEnv(base *SmokeConfig, envPath string) (*SmokeConfig, error) {
330		envCfg, err := Load(envPath)
331		if err != nil {
332			return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading env config %s: %w", envPath, err)
333		}
334	
335		// Deep merge: env settings override base
336		if envCfg.Settings.Timeout.Duration > 0 {
337			base.Settings.Timeout = envCfg.Settings.Timeout
338		}
339		if envCfg.Settings.FailFast {
340			base.Settings.FailFast = true
341		}
342		if envCfg.Settings.Parallel {
343			base.Settings.Parallel = true
344		}
345	
346		// Prepend env prereqs (they run before base prereqs)
347		base.Prereqs = append(envCfg.Prereqs, base.Prereqs...)
348	
349		// Append env tests (they run after base tests)
350		base.Tests = append(base.Tests, envCfg.Tests...)
351	
352		return base, nil
353	}
354

content.subject: Implement CheckOTelTrace with Jaeger polling
content.description: Create assertion_otel.go with Jaeger API polling (500ms interval, min_spans check). 5 tests with httptest mock.

content.subject: Inject traceparent into HTTP requests
content.description: Add CheckHTTPWithTrace wrapper that injects traceparent header. Test with httptest.

content.subject: Add validation for otel config fields
content.description: Validate jaeger_url required when otel enabled, URL format, add otel_trace to standalone assertions.

content.subject: Add OTelConfig + OTelTraceCheck to schema
content.description: Add OTelConfig struct to SmokeConfig, OTelTraceCheck to Expect, Headers to WebSocketCheck, Metadata to GRPCHealthCheck. Write parsing test.

content.subject: Update CLAUDE.md and self-smoke config
content.description: Add otel_trace to assertion table (27 total). Update CLAUDE.md with otel config docs. Update self-smoke if applicable.

content.subject: Inject traceparent into WebSocket handshake
content.description: Add CheckWebSocketWithTrace wrapper. Add Headers field to WebSocketCheck if needed. Test with mock WS server.

content.subject: Inject traceparent into gRPC metadata
content.description: Add CheckGRPCHealthWithTrace. Add Metadata field to GRPCHealthCheck. Stub version for non-grpc builds.

content.subject: Add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags
content.description: Add CLI flags to cmd/run.go. --otel-collector overrides jaeger_url and enables otel. --no-otel disables it. 2 tests.

content.subject: Implement TraceContext + SpanContext
content.description: Create trace.go with W3C traceparent generation (crypto/rand, no SDK). 6 tests covering format, uniqueness, disabled state.

content.subject: Wire trace context into runner pipeline
content.description: Init TraceContext in Runner.Run(), create per-test SpanContext, dispatch WithTrace variants for HTTP/gRPC/WS, dispatch otel_trace assertion. Integration test.

content.subject: Implement CheckOTelTrace with Jaeger polling
content.description: Create assertion_otel.go with Jaeger API polling (500ms interval, min_spans check). 5 tests with httptest mock.

content.subject: Inject traceparent into HTTP requests
content.description: Add CheckHTTPWithTrace wrapper that injects traceparent header. Test with httptest.

content.subject: Add validation for otel config fields
content.description: Validate jaeger_url required when otel enabled, URL format, add otel_trace to standalone assertions.

content.subject: Add OTelConfig + OTelTraceCheck to schema
content.description: Add OTelConfig struct to SmokeConfig, OTelTraceCheck to Expect, Headers to WebSocketCheck, Metadata to GRPCHealthCheck. Write parsing test.

content.description: Add otel_trace to assertion table (27 total). Update CLAUDE.md with otel config docs. Update self-smoke if applicable.

content.subject: Inject traceparent into WebSocket handshake
content.description: Add CheckWebSocketWithTrace wrapper. Add Headers field to WebSocketCheck if needed. Test with mock WS server.

content.description: Add CLI flags to cmd/run.go. --otel-collector overrides jaeger_url and enables otel. --no-otel disables it. 2 tests.

**Task 6: Inject traceparent into WebSocket handshake**

I see the WebSocket implementation builds the upgrade request manually in `wsUpgrade`. I need to modify `wsUpgrade` to accept headers, and add `CheckWebSocketWithTrace`.

Host: %s

Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Key: %s

content.text: **Task 7: Inject traceparent into gRPC metadata**

content.content:
1	//go:build grpc
2	
3	package runner
4	
5	import (
6		"context"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/internal/schema"
10		"google.golang.org/grpc"
11		"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials"
12		"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure"
13		healthpb "google.golang.org/grpc/health/grpc_health_v1"
14	)
15	
16	// CheckGRPCHealth queries grpc.health.v1.Health/Check and passes if SERVING.
17	func CheckGRPCHealth(check *schema.GRPCHealthCheck) AssertionResult {
18		timeout := check.Timeout.Duration
19		if timeout == 0 {
20			timeout = 5 * time.Second
21		}
22		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)
23		defer cancel()
24	
25		var creds credentials.TransportCredentials
26		if check.UseTLS {
27			creds = credentials.NewTLS(nil)
28		} else {
29			creds = insecure.NewCredentials()
30		}
31	
32		conn, err := grpc.NewClient(check.Address, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(creds))
33		if err != nil {
34			return AssertionResult{
35				Type:     "grpc_health",
36				Expected: check.Address,
37				Actual:   "dial error: " + err.Error(),
38				Passed:   false,
39			}
40		}
41		defer conn.Close()
42	
43		client := healthpb.NewHealthClient(conn)
44		resp, err := client.Check(ctx, &healthpb.HealthCheckRequest{Service: check.Service})
45		if err != nil {
46			return AssertionResult{
47				Type:     "grpc_health",
48				Expected: "SERVING",
49				Actual:   "rpc error: " + err.Error(),
50				Passed:   false,
51			}
52		}
53	
54		status := resp.GetStatus().String()
55		return AssertionResult{
56			Type:     "grpc_health",
57			Expected: "SERVING",
58			Actual:   status,
59			Passed:   status == "SERVING",
60		}
61	}
62

file.content:
package cmd

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"os/signal"
	"path/filepath"
	"syscall"
	"time"

	"github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/internal/monorepo"
	"github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/internal/reporter"
	"github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/internal/runner"
	"github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/internal/schema"
	"github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

var runCmd = &cobra.Command{
	Use:   "run",
	Short: "Run smoke tests",
	Long:  "Execute smoke tests defined in .smoke.yaml",
	RunE:  runSmoke,
}

var (
	configFile  string
	tags        []string
	excludeTags []string
	format      string
	failFast    bool
	timeout     string
	dryRun      bool
	watch       bool
	envName     string
	monorepoMode bool
)

func init() {
	rootCmd.AddCommand(runCmd)
	runCmd.Flags().StringVarP(&configFile, "file", "f", ".smoke.yaml", "Config file path")
	runCmd.Flags().StringSliceVar(&tags, "tag", nil, "Include only tests with these tags")
	runCmd.Flags().StringSliceVar(&excludeTags, "exclude-tag", nil, "Exclude tests with these tags")
	runCmd.Flags().StringVar(&format, "format", "terminal", "Output format (terminal|json|junit|tap|prometheus)")
	runCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&failFast, "fail-fast", false, "Stop on first failure")
	runCmd.Flags().StringVar(&timeout, "timeout", "", "Per-test timeout override (e.g. 30s)")
	runCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&dryRun, "dry-run", false, "List tests without running")
	runCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&watch, "watch", false, "Re-run tests when files change (Ctrl+C to exit)")
	runCmd.Flags().StringVar(&envName, "env", "", "Load environment-specific config (e.g. staging loads staging.smoke.yaml)")
	runCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&monorepoMode, "monorepo", false, "Auto-discover .smoke.yaml in subdirectories")

# cosmo-smoke — Project Instructions

## Overview

Universal smoke test runner. Standalone Go binary that reads `.smoke.yaml` and runs lightweight smoke tests. Designed for CosmoLabs' ~95-project portfolio.

**Repository**: `github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke`
**Company**: CosmoLabs
**Version**: 0.7.0

## Architecture

```
cmd/
├── root.go          # Cobra root command with banner
├── run.go           # smoke run — main entry point
├── init_cmd.go      # smoke init — auto-detect + generate config
└── version.go       # smoke version (ldflags-injected)
internal/
├── schema/          # SmokeConfig structs, YAML parsing, validation
├── runner/          # Assertion engine (26 types), prereq runner, test execution
├── reporter/        # Terminal (Lipgloss) + JSON reporters
├── monorepo/        # Sub-config discovery for monorepo projects
└── detector/        # Project type detection + template generation
```

## Key Design Decisions

- **Minimal deps**: Cobra + Lipgloss + yaml.v3 + gjson. No Viper, no Bubbletea.
- **Pure assertions**: All 26 assertion types are pure functions — no side effects.
- **Config inheritance**: `includes:` directive + Go templates (`{{ .Env.FOO }}`).
- **Config-dir-relative**: Commands execute from the config file's directory, not cwd.
- **All errors at once**: Validation returns all errors, not just the first.
- **Reporter interface**: Terminal and JSON reporters are pluggable via interface.
- **Watch mode**: `--watch` keeps smoke resident and re-runs on file changes. fsnotify-backed. 500ms debounce.
- **Retry**: Opt-in `retry: {count, backoff}` on test level. Exponential backoff. No side effects on pass-first-try.
- **Monorepo**: `--monorepo` flag auto-discovers `.smoke.yaml` in subdirectories. Unlimited depth, configurable exclusions.
- **WebSocket**: Stdlib-only WebSocket client. Connect-send-expect pattern with no external deps.
- **gRPC opt-in**: gRPC health check excluded from default build. Use `-tags grpc` to include.

## Build & Test

```bash
go build ./...                    # Build
go test ./...                     # Run all tests (253 total)
smoke run                         # Self-smoke (6 tests)
go build -ldflags "-s -w -X github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/cmd.Version=X.Y.Z" -o smoke .
```

## Commands

```bash
smoke run [--tag X] [--exclude-tag X] [--format terminal|json|junit|tap|prometheus] [--fail-fast] [--timeout 30s] [-f path] [--dry-run] [--watch] [--monorepo]
smoke init [--force] [--from-running CONTAINER]
smoke version
```

## Assertion Types

| Type | Field | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| exit_code | `*int` | Process exit code match |
| stdout_contains | `string` | Substring match on stdout |
| stdout_matches | `string` | Regex match on stdout |
| stderr_contains | `string` | Substring match on stderr |
| stderr_matches | `string` | Regex match on stderr |
| file_exists | `string` | File exists relative to config dir |
| env_exists | `string` | Environment variable exists |
| port_listening | `{port, protocol?, host?}` | TCP/UDP port is open |
| process_running | `string` | Named process currently running (pgrep -x / tasklist) |
| http | `{url, method?, status_code?, body_contains?, body_matches?, header_contains?}` | HTTP endpoint check |
| json_field | `{path, equals?, contains?, matches?}` | JSONPath assertion on stdout |
| response_time_ms | `*int` | Test duration must not exceed this threshold |
| ssl_cert | `{host, port?, min_days_remaining?, allow_self_signed?}` | TLS cert valid + expiry threshold |
| redis_ping | `{host?, port?, password?}` | Redis PING returns +PONG (RESP protocol) |
| memcached_version | `{host?, port?}` | Memcached `version` command returns VERSION |
| postgres_ping | `{host?, port?}` | Postgres server SSLRequest handshake returns valid protocol byte |
| mysql_ping | `{host?, port?}` | MySQL server sends valid v10 handshake packet on connection |
| grpc_health | `{address, service?, use_tls?, timeout?}` | grpc.health.v1 Health/Check returns SERVING (requires `-tags grpc`) |
| websocket | `{url, send?, expect_contains?, expect_matches?, timeout?}` | WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion |
| docker_container_running | `{name}` | Named Docker container is currently running |
| docker_image_exists | `{image}` | Docker image exists locally |
| url_reachable | `{url, timeout?, status_code?}` | HTTP/HTTPS connectivity check |
| service_reachable | `{url, timeout?}` | External service dependency check |
| s3_bucket | `{bucket, region?, endpoint?}` | S3-compatible bucket accessibility (anonymous HEAD) |
| version_check | `{command, pattern}` | Tool version verification via shell command + regex |

Plus `allow_failure: true` on Test for flaky/allowed-failure tests.

## Output Formats

`smoke run --format X` supports: `terminal` (default), `json`, `junit`, `tap`, `prometheus`.

## Detected Project Types

Go, Node (bun/npm), Python, Docker, Rust — each with tailored smoke test templates.

## OpenTelemetry Integration

# cosmo-smoke — Project Instructions

## Overview

Universal smoke test runner. Standalone Go binary that reads `.smoke.yaml` and runs lightweight smoke tests. Designed for CosmoLabs' ~95-project portfolio.

**Repository**: `github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke`
**Company**: CosmoLabs
**Version**: 0.7.0

## Architecture

```
cmd/
├── root.go          # Cobra root command with banner
├── run.go           # smoke run — main entry point
├── init_cmd.go      # smoke init — auto-detect + generate config
└── version.go       # smoke version (ldflags-injected)
internal/
├── schema/          # SmokeConfig structs, YAML parsing, validation
├── runner/          # Assertion engine (26 types), prereq runner, test execution
├── reporter/        # Terminal (Lipgloss) + JSON reporters
├── monorepo/        # Sub-config discovery for monorepo projects
└── detector/        # Project type detection + template generation
```

## Key Design Decisions

- **Minimal deps**: Cobra + Lipgloss + yaml.v3 + gjson. No Viper, no Bubbletea.
- **Pure assertions**: All 26 assertion types are pure functions — no side effects.
- **Config inheritance**: `includes:` directive + Go templates (`{{ .Env.FOO }}`).
- **Config-dir-relative**: Commands execute from the config file's directory, not cwd.
- **All errors at once**: Validation returns all errors, not just the first.
- **Reporter interface**: Terminal and JSON reporters are pluggable via interface.
- **Watch mode**: `--watch` keeps smoke resident and re-runs on file changes. fsnotify-backed. 500ms debounce.
- **Retry**: Opt-in `retry: {count, backoff}` on test level. Exponential backoff. No side effects on pass-first-try.
- **Monorepo**: `--monorepo` flag auto-discovers `.smoke.yaml` in subdirectories. Unlimited depth, configurable exclusions.
- **WebSocket**: Stdlib-only WebSocket client. Connect-send-expect pattern with no external deps.
- **gRPC opt-in**: gRPC health check excluded from default build. Use `-tags grpc` to include.

## Build & Test

```bash
go build ./...                    # Build
go test ./...                     # Run all tests (253 total)
smoke run                         # Self-smoke (6 tests)
go build -ldflags "-s -w -X github.com/CosmoLabs-org/cosmo-smoke/cmd.Version=X.Y.Z" -o smoke .
```

## Commands

```bash
smoke run [--tag X] [--exclude-tag X] [--format terminal|json|junit|tap|prometheus] [--fail-fast] [--timeout 30s] [-f path] [--dry-run] [--watch] [--monorepo] [--otel-collector URL] [--no-otel]
smoke init [--force] [--from-running CONTAINER]
smoke version
```

## Assertion Types

| Type | Field | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| exit_code | `*int` | Process exit code match |
| stdout_contains | `string` | Substring match on stdout |
| stdout_matches | `string` | Regex match on stdout |
| stderr_contains | `string` | Substring match on stderr |
| stderr_matches | `string` | Regex match on stderr |
| file_exists | `string` | File exists relative to config dir |
| env_exists | `string` | Environment variable exists |
| port_listening | `{port, protocol?, host?}` | TCP/UDP port is open |
| process_running | `string` | Named process currently running (pgrep -x / tasklist) |
| http | `{url, method?, status_code?, body_contains?, body_matches?, header_contains?}` | HTTP endpoint check |
| json_field | `{path, equals?, contains?, matches?}` | JSONPath assertion on stdout |
| response_time_ms | `*int` | Test duration must not exceed this threshold |
| ssl_cert | `{host, port?, min_days_remaining?, allow_self_signed?}` | TLS cert valid + expiry threshold |
| redis_ping | `{host?, port?, password?}` | Redis PING returns +PONG (RESP protocol) |
| memcached_version | `{host?, port?}` | Memcached `version` command returns VERSION |
| postgres_ping | `{host?, port?}` | Postgres server SSLRequest handshake returns valid protocol byte |
| mysql_ping | `{host?, port?}` | MySQL server sends valid v10 handshake packet on connection |
| grpc_health | `{address, service?, use_tls?, timeout?}` | grpc.health.v1 Health/Check returns SERVING (requires `-tags grpc`) |
| websocket | `{url, send?, expect_contains?, expect_matches?, timeout?}` | WebSocket connect-send-expect assertion |
| docker_container_running | `{name}` | Named Docker container is currently running |
| docker_image_exists | `{image}` | Docker image exists locally |
| url_reachable | `{url, timeout?, status_code?}` | HTTP/HTTPS connectivity check |
| service_reachable | `{url, timeout?}` | External service dependency check |
| s3_bucket | `{bucket, region?, endpoint?}` | S3-compatible bucket accessibility (anonymous HEAD) |
| version_check | `{command, pattern}` | Tool version verification via shell command + regex |
| otel_trace | `{jaeger_url, service_name?, min_spans?, timeout?}` | Jaeger API trace verification (W3C traceparent propagation) |

Plus `allow_failure: true` on Test for flaky/allowed-failure tests.

## OpenTelemetry Integration

```yaml
otel:
  enabled: true
  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"
  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"
  trace_propagation: true
```

When enabled, W3C `traceparent` headers are auto-injected into HTTP, gRPC, and WebSocket assertions. The `otel_trace` assertion verifies traces arrived at a Jaeger-compatible collector.

## Output Formats

`smoke run --format X` supports: `terminal` (default), `json`, `junit`, `tap`, `prometheus`.

## Detected Project Types

Go, Node (bun/npm), Python, Docker, Rust — each with tailored smoke test templates.

├── monorepo/        # Sub-config discovery for monorepo projects
└── detector/        # Project type detection + template generation

content.description: Create assertion_otel.go with Jaeger API polling (500ms interval, min_spans check). 5 tests with httptest mock.

content.subject: Inject traceparent into HTTP requests

content.subject: Add validation for otel config fields
content.description: Validate jaeger_url required when otel enabled, URL format, add otel_trace to standalone assertions.

content.subject: Update CLAUDE.md and self-smoke config

content.subject: Inject traceparent into WebSocket handshake

content.subject: Inject traceparent into gRPC metadata

content.subject: Add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags
content.description: Add CLI flags to cmd/run.go. --otel-collector overrides jaeger_url and enables otel. --no-otel disables it. 2 tests.

content.subject: Create trace context generation (trace.go + trace_test.go)
content.description: Create internal/runner/trace.go with W3C trace context generation and internal/runner/trace_test.go with 6 tests.

content.subject: Create OTel trace assertion (assertion_otel.go + assertion_otel_test.go)
content.description: Create internal/runner/assertion_otel.go with Jaeger API trace verification and internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go with 5 tests.

content.subject: Run tests and verify all pass
content.description: Run go test for the new trace and otel files, verify all 11 tests pass.
content.activeForm: Running and verifying all tests pass

content.subject: Implement TraceContext + SpanContext
content.description: Create trace.go with W3C traceparent generation (crypto/rand, no SDK). 6 tests covering format, uniqueness, disabled state.

content.subject: Wire trace context into runner pipeline
content.description: Init TraceContext in Runner.Run(), create per-test SpanContext, dispatch WithTrace variants for HTTP/gRPC/WS, dispatch otel_trace assertion. Integration test.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/gab/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.0.2/skills/verification-before-completion

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence ≠ evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter ≠ compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion ≠ excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
✅ [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
❌ "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
✅ Write → Run (pass) → Revert fix → Run (MUST FAIL) → Restore → Run (pass)
❌ "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
✅ [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
❌ "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
✅ Re-read plan → Create checklist → Verify each → Report gaps or completion
❌ "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
✅ Agent reports success → Check VCS diff → Verify changes → Report actual state
❌ Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion → redirect → rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.

**What was built:**

- **5 roadmap items**: ROAD-034 (active) through ROAD-038 (future)

**Commits:**

content.text:
# Session End

Orchestrates end-of-session tasks by delegating to the session-end SOP.

## Usage

```bash
/session-end                    # Interactive (auto-detects trivial/repeat)
/session-end --quick            # Trivial changes: commit only, no docs
/session-end --commit-only      # Just commit, no summary
/session-end --continue         # Continue work: asks lean vs full if prompt exists
/session-end --lean             # Explicit lean: commit + prompt status only
/session-end --complete         # Feature complete, ready to merge
/session-end --release          # Complete + trigger release (master only)
/session-end --skip-summary     # Skip session summary generation
/session-end --status           # Check if session-end was run (no side effects)
/session-end --amend            # Force amend mode (update existing artifacts)
/session-end --amend --release  # Amend + re-bump version
/session-end --force            # Bypass amend detection, run fresh
/session-end patch              # Session-end + patch release (bugfixes, settings)
/session-end minor              # Session-end + minor release (new features)
/session-end major              # Session-end + major release (breaking changes)
```

Natural language also works: "just commit", "continue tomorrow", "feature complete", "with release", "lean mode", "patch up version bump", "minor release"

## Step 1: Parse Mode

| Input | Mode |
|-------|------|
| (no args) | full |
| `--quick`, "just commit", "trivial" | quick |
| `--commit-only` | commit-only |
| `--continue`, "continue tomorrow" | continue |
| `--lean`, "lean mode" | lean |
| `--complete`, "feature complete" | complete |
| `--release`, "with release" | release |
| `--skip-summary` | skip-summary |
| `--status` | status |
| `--amend`, "amend mode" | amend |
| `--amend --release` | amend-release |
| `--force` | force (bypass amend detection) |
| `patch` | patch |
| `minor`, "minor release" | minor |
| `major`, "major release" | major |

## Step 2: Status Check

If mode=status: run `ccs session-status`, display output, **EXIT**.

## Step 2.5: Amend Detection

Check if session-end was already run this session:
```bash
ccs session-status --json
```

If the JSON shows session summary phase with status "✅" (already completed):
1. Print: `⚠️ Session-end already ran. Running in **amend mode** — will update existing artifacts.`
2. Find existing artifacts from today:
   ```bash
   summary=$(ls docs/sessions/Session-*$(date +%Y-%m-%d)*.md 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
   release_notes=$(ls docs/release-notes/*$(date +%Y-%m-%d)*.md 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
   continuation=$(ls docs/prompts/*$(date +%Y-%m-%d)*continuation*.md 2>/dev/null | tail -1)
   ```
3. Pass artifact paths to Phase 3 agents (amend-aware prompts below)

If `--force` flag → skip amend detection, run fresh.

**Amend-aware agent prompts:**

Session summary agent (amend mode):
```
An existing session summary was written earlier this session: {summary_path}
Read it. Append a "## Continued Work" section with new commits and accomplishments.
Don't rewrite from scratch — add to the existing document.
New commits since last session-end: {git log --oneline since last commit}
```

Continuation prompt agent (amend mode):
```
An existing continuation prompt was written earlier: {continuation_path}
Read it. Mark completed goals as [x]. Add new goals from continued work.
Update "What Got Done" section. Don't create a new file — update the existing one.
```

**Amend mode Phase 4**: Skip release unless `--amend --release` is explicitly passed.

## Step 3: Worktree Redirect

```bash
git rev-parse --git-common-dir  # If != ".git", it's a worktree
```

If worktree detected:
- quick/commit-only → handle inline (just /commit-all)
- continue/lean → invoke `/worktree-end --continue` via Skill tool
- complete → invoke `/worktree-end --complete` via Skill tool
- All others → invoke `/worktree-end` via Skill tool

Report: `🌿 Worktree detected — redirecting to /worktree-end [mode]`

**Do NOT continue after redirect.**

## Step 4: Create Phase Checklist (MANDATORY)

**Before executing ANY phase**, create tasks for every active phase. This gives the user real-time visibility and prevents phase skipping.

Use the mode-phase matrix to determine which phases are active:

**Full/complete/release mode tasks:**
```
TaskCreate: "Phase 0+1: Pre-commit hygiene (ccs session-end-pre)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.5: Code simplification (/simplify)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.75: Verification gate (superpowers:verification-before-completion)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.8: Code review gate (superpowers:code-reviewer)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.9: Integration check (optional, manifest-gated)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 2: Commit changes (/commit-all)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3: Parallel blast (post-commit)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3c: Idea capture (observations from session)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 4: Release decision (uses blast output)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 6: Finalize (ccs session-end-finalize)"
```

**Quick/commit-only mode tasks:**
```
TaskCreate: "Phase 2: Commit changes (/commit-all)"
```

**Lean mode tasks:**
```
TaskCreate: "Phase 2: Commit changes (/commit-all)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 6.1: Update prompt status"
```

**Continue mode tasks:**
```
TaskCreate: "Phase 0+1: Pre-commit hygiene"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.75: Verification gate (superpowers:verification-before-completion)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.8: Code review gate (superpowers:code-reviewer)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 1.9: Integration check (optional, manifest-gated)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 2: Commit changes (/commit-all)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3: Parallel blast (post-commit)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3c: Idea capture (observations from session)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 3d: Tool reflection (/reflect)"
TaskCreate: "Phase 4: Release decision"
TaskCreate: "Phase 6: Finalize (ccs session-end-finalize)"
```

## Step 5: Execute Phases

**For each phase task, you MUST:**
1. Mark the task `in_progress`
2. Announce: `**Phase N: [name]**`
3. Execute the phase
4. Show the result to the user
5. Mark the task `completed`

If a phase is not applicable (e.g., no code files changed → skip simplify), announce it:
`**Phase 1.5: Code simplification** — skipped (no code files changed)`
and mark the task completed with a note.

### Sequential Phases (before commit)

| Phase | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **0+1** | `ccs session-end-pre` → read JSON → report findings. Extract `change_profile` from JSON for phase gating. Also run `ccs workcheck` to verify goal completion and feed the continuation prompt with gap analysis. |
| **1.5** | Invoke `/simplify` via Skill tool. **Gate:** Skip if `change_profile.category` is `docs-only`, `config-only`, or `test-only`. Only run on `source`/`mixed`. |
| **1.75** | **Verification gate** — conditional on `change_profile.category`: skip if `docs-only` or `config-only` ("no source changes"); skip if `test-only` ("already verified"); if `source`/`mixed` with `has_untested_source=true`, run scoped tests on `go_packages_touched` only (not `./...`); otherwise skip ("all changes already tested"). |
| **1.8** | **Code review gate**: If `change_profile.category` is `source` or `mixed`, dispatch `superpowers:code-reviewer` agent on `git diff HEAD`. Review high-confidence findings (>80). Fix critical issues before committing. Skip if `docs-only`, `config-only`, or `test-only`. |
| **1.9** | **Integration check (optional)**: If `integration-manifest.yaml` exists and session touched files in 2+ platform directories (check git log), offer: `🔍 Multi-platform session detected — run integration check?` with options "Run quick check (`ccs audit integration --quick`)" / "Run docs check (`ccs audit integration --docs`)" / "Skip". Use AskUserQuestion. Skip silently if no manifest exists. |
| **2** | Invoke `/commit-all` via Skill tool |

### Phase 2.25: Conditional Rebuild

Based on `change_profile` from Phase 0+1:

```
IF change_profile.cli_changed == true:
  → `ccs rebuild --only ccs`

ELIF changed files touch tools/openrouter/:
  → `ccs rebuild --only openrouter`

ELIF changed files touch tools/cosmohooks/:
  → `ccs rebuild --only cosmohooks`

ELSE:
  → skip: "Rebuild — skipped (no binary-affecting changes)"
```

### Phase 2.75: Auto-Stage Changelog

Scan commits since last tag for changelog-worthy entries:
```bash
git log --oneline $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)..HEAD --format="%s"
```

For each commit matching conventional format (`--type` is **required**):
- `feat(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type added`
- `fix(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type fixed`
- `refactor(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type changed`
- `perf(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type changed`
- `security(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type security`
- `deprecate(*)`: → `ccs changelog add "subject" --type deprecated`
- Breaking changes: add `--breaking` flag
- Skip: `chore`, `docs`, `test`, `ci`, `style`

**CCS changelog add usage:** `ccs changelog add "description" --type {added|fixed|changed|deprecated|removed|security} [--breaking]`

**Dedupe semantics (FB-485):** Always scan every commit since the last tag and call `ccs changelog add` for each. Let the tool dedupe internally — do NOT pre-check with `ccs changelog status`. `AddEntry` in `internal/changelog/staging.go` enforces two dedup rules:

| Rule | Matches on | Effect |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Source-based | Non-"manual" source (e.g. `FEAT-034`, `BUG-176`) matching an existing entry's Source | Returns error `entry with source X already exists` |
| Description-based | Case-insensitive trimmed description matching an existing entry | Returns error `duplicate entry already exists` |

Treat both errors as **expected non-failures**. Count them as "skipped (already staged)" and continue scanning. Only log unexpected errors (I/O, malformed YAML).

Display:
```
Auto-staged N changelog entries:
  ✨ added: X
  🐛 fixed: Y
  (A skipped: already staged | B skipped: chore/docs/test)
```

If no feat/fix/refactor commits found, skip silently. If the scan runs on a session where entries were manually staged beforehand, the two sets merge cleanly via the dedupe rules above.

### Phase 2.5: Task List Preservation (before Phase 3)

If tasks exist in the current session's TaskList:

1. Read all tasks (completed, in_progress, pending, failed)
2. Build a task summary table:
   ```
   | Status | Count |
   |--------|-------|
   | Completed | N |
   | In Progress | N |
   | Pending | N |
   | Failed | N |
   ```
3. Include this table in the session summary data (passed to Phase 3 agents)
4. If any tasks are **incomplete** (in_progress or pending), flag them for the continuation prompt:
   - Store the list of incomplete task descriptions for `/continuation-prompt` to pick up
   - These become carry-over tasks in the next session's prompt

**Skip if no tasks exist.** This phase is a data collection step — it does not modify tasks.

### Phase 3a: CCS Commands (instant)

Run all CCS commands in parallel via Bash:
```bash
ccs session-end-post --json     # Post-audit findings
ccs doc-audit --json            # Documentation gaps
ccs roadmap reconcile --json    # Superseded roadmap items
ccs verify review --brief       # SVQ status
ccs memwatch report             # Memory usage report (Memwatchtower)
```

### Phase 3b: Sonnet Agents (background)

Dispatch in a SINGLE message with `run_in_background: true`:
- Agent A: Invoke `/session-summary` (or amend existing summary if amend mode)
- Agent B: Invoke `/continuation-prompt` (or update existing prompt if amend mode)
- Agent C: Doc-gap fixer (Sonnet) — see below. **Skip if no new/modified `cmd/*.go` files this session.**

**Agent C prompt template:**
```
You are filling documentation gaps for CCS commands modified this session.

1. Run `ccs doc-audit --json` to get all gaps
2. Run `git diff --name-only HEAD~N..HEAD` (N = session commit count) to get modified files
3. Filter doc-audit gaps to ONLY commands whose source file (tools/ccsession/cmd/<name>.go) was modified this session
4. For each filtered gap with "README" in missing_from:
   - Read the command source for usage/flags
   - Run `ccs <name> --help` for description
   - Generate `READMEs/commands/README.<name>.md` following the pattern of existing READMEs (see READMEs/commands/README.audit.md as template)
5. Stage the new files (do NOT commit — main thread handles commits)
6. Report: "Generated N READMEs for: <list>"

Constraints: Only generate for commands MODIFIED this session. Do NOT touch the other 100+ undocumented commands — that is a separate refactor.
```

**Do NOT wait for Phase 3b to proceed to Phase 4.** You'll be notified when agents complete.

### Phase 4: Release Decision (after CCS only)

**Do NOT wait for Phase 3b agents.** CCS output from 3a is all that's needed.

**This phase is a DETERMINISTIC SCRIPT, not a judgment call.**

```
1. Run `ccs version --release-check --json`
2. Parse JSON result

3. IF hold_active AND hold condition matches work done this session:
   → Run `ccs version --unblock "<condition text>"`
   → Run `ccs version --unhold`
   → Re-run `ccs version --release-check --json`

4. IF mode=release/patch/minor/major:
   → type = that explicit type (override suggested_type)
   ELSE:
   → type = suggested_type from JSON

5. IF all_conditions_met=true AND type is set AND hold_active=false:
   → EXECUTE `ccs version --bump <type> --highlights "..."`
   → This is LOCAL and REVERSIBLE. There is NO reason to defer.
   → You are NOT asking permission. The SOP authorizes this action.

6. IF NOT all_conditions_met:
   → Show why and move on

7. IF mode=amend AND --release NOT passed:
   → Skip release entirely
```

**POST-PHASE ASSERTION:** If release-check showed `all_conditions_met=true` but no version bump was executed, you have violated this SOP. Go back and execute step 5 NOW, or state a concrete technical blocker (not "should we wait" — a real blocker like failing tests or missing changelog).

Version bumps are local commits. They are reversible via `git reset`. "Deferring to next session" is NOT acceptable when conditions are met — context will be lost and the user will have to re-engage.

### Phase 3b.complete: Agent Output

When Sonnet agents complete (you'll be notified), commit their output. If amend mode, verify amendments look correct (appended, not duplicated).

**While agents run**, collect CCS output and review:
- Roadmap items in-progress — assess if any should be updated
- Ideas, lessons, feedback — file if warranted

**Present consolidated dashboard when all complete:**
```
Phase 3: Parallel Blast
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✅ Post-audit: N findings           [CCS — Phase 3a]
✅ Doc gaps: N undocumented         [CCS — Phase 3a]
✅ Roadmap: N items reconciled      [CCS — Phase 3a]
✅ SVQ: N pending items             [CCS — Phase 3a]
✅ Session summary                  [Agent A — Phase 3b]
✅ Continuation prompt              [Agent B — Phase 3b]
✅ Doc-gap READMEs (N generated)    [Agent C — Phase 3b, skipped if no cmd changes]
```

### Phase 3c: Idea Capture (mandatory in full/complete/release modes)

Before finalizing, reflect on ALL observations from the session — code review findings, simplify suggestions, patterns noticed, problems spotted, and ideas generated during implementation.

**Steps:**
1. Collect observations from:
   - Code review (Phase 1.8) low-priority findings that weren't fixed
   - Simplify (Phase 1.5) suggestions that were deferred
   - Implementation work — patterns, problems, or improvements noticed
   - Post-audit (Phase 3a) findings worth tracking
   - Any "we should..." or "it would be nice if..." moments from the session

2. Present to user as a categorized list:
   ```
   💡 Session observations:
   
   IDEAS:
   - [description] → would file as: ccs idea add "title"
   
   BUGS:
   - [description] → would file as: /bug "title"
   
   IMPROVEMENTS:
   - [description] → would file as: ccs feedback send . "title"
   ```

3. For each item the user approves (or all if user says "file all"):
   - **Ideas**: `ccs idea add "title" --body "full context from session"`
   - **Bugs**: Use `/bug` skill with `--description`, `--severity`, `--fix-direction`, `--affected-files`
   - **Improvements**: `ccs feedback send . "title" --body="context"`
   - Include enough session context that a future session can act on it without re-discovery

4. If no observations worth filing → announce: `**Phase 3c: Idea Capture** — skipped (no actionable observations)`

**Gate:** Skip in quick, commit-only, and lean modes. Active in full, complete, release, and continue modes.

### Phase 3d: Tool Reflection

```
Phase 3d: Tool Reflection
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Review tools, SOPs, and skills used this session.
Any gaps, friction, or problems worth feeding back to CCS?
```

Invoke `/reflect` via Skill tool. It will self-review tool usage and auto-send any actionable feedback.

**Gate:** Skip in quick, lean, and commit-only modes. Active in full, complete, release, and continue modes.

### Phase 6: Finalize

| Phase | Action |
|-------|--------|
| **6** | `ccs session-end-finalize` |

## Step 6: Mark Status Line (MANDATORY)

**After SOP completes (regardless of mode), ALWAYS run:**
```bash
cosmohooks mark-session-end
```

This flips `◻ /session-end` → `✅ /session-end` in the status line. The SOP also calls this, but this step is a failsafe — run it even if you think it already ran. Idempotent.

## Related

`/commit-all` - Multi-commit workflow
`/continuation-prompt` - Handoff for next session
`/release` - Version management
`/worktree-end` - Worktree session lifecycle
`/efficiency-check` - Full efficiency analysis

input.prompt:
Write a session summary for the cosmo-smoke project at `/Users/gab/PROJECTS/cosmo-smoke`.

This session implemented the full OpenTelemetry trace correlation feature (FEAT-012, IDEA-MO1FBPJB).

Key accomplishments:
1. Triaged ideas, chose OpenTelemetry trace correlation for brainstorming
2. Designed the feature via interactive Q&A: global otel config block, W3C traceparent propagation, Jaeger API verification
3. Wrote design spec: docs/brainstorming/2026-04-18-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md
4. Spec reviewed (6 issues found, all fixed)
5. Wrote implementation plan: docs/planning-mode/2026-04-19-opentelemetry-trace-correlation.md (10 tasks, 6 chunks)
6. Plan reviewed (3 issues found, all fixed)
7. Generated continuation prompt + GLM dispatch manifest
8. Executed all 10 implementation tasks:
   - Added OTelConfig + OTelTraceCheck to schema with validation
   - Created TraceContext + SpanContext (W3C traceparent generation)
   - Created CheckOTelTrace with Jaeger API polling
   - Injected traceparent into HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC assertions
   - Wired trace context into runner pipeline
   - Added --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags
   - Updated CLAUDE.md (27 assertion types)
9. Created FEAT-012 issue, ROAD-034 through ROAD-038 roadmap items
10. All 269 tests pass, 6/6 self-smoke pass, build clean

Files created:
- internal/runner/trace.go, trace_test.go
- internal/runner/assertion_otel.go, assertion_otel_test.go

Files modified:
- internal/schema/schema.go, validate.go (and tests)
- internal/runner/runner.go, assertion_network.go, assertion_ws.go, assertion_grpc.go, assertion_grpc_stub.go (and tests)
- cmd/run.go, CLAUDE.md

Write the summary to: docs/sessions/Session-2026-04-19-v0.8-OpenTelemetry-Trace-Correlation.md

Format: Follow the pattern of existing session summaries in docs/sessions/. Include: title, date, version, goals, what got done, commits, metrics (tests, files), next steps.

- [x] Task 1: Add OTelConfig + OTelTraceCheck to schema
- [x] Task 2: Add validation for otel config fields
- [x] Task 3: Implement TraceContext + SpanContext
- [x] Task 4: Implement CheckOTelTrace with Jaeger polling
- [x] Task 5: Inject traceparent into HTTP requests
- [x] Task 6: Inject traceparent into WebSocket handshake
- [x] Task 7: Inject traceparent into gRPC metadata
- [x] Task 8: Wire trace context into runner pipeline
- [x] Task 9: Add --otel-collector and --no-otel CLI flags
- [x] Task 10: Update CLAUDE.md and self-smoke config

Update the frontmatter:

attachment.snippet:
1	---
2	completed: "2026-04-19"
3	created: "2026-04-18"
4	status: COMPLETED
5	tags:
6	    - opentelemetry
7	    - tracing
8	    - observability
9	    - jaeger
10	title: OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation for cosmo-smoke
11	---
12	
13	# OpenTelemetry Trace Correlation
14	
15	## Problem
16	
17	Smoke tests verify infrastructure health but are invisible to observability pipelines. When a smoke test fails, there's no trace in Jaeger/Tempo to correlate with. Teams can't answer: "did our smoke test traffic actually generate traces?" or "is the trace pipeline working end-to-end?"
18	
19	## Solution
20	
21	Add W3C trace context propagation to cosmo-smoke's network assertions (HTTP, gRPC, WebSocket) and a dedicated `otel_trace` assertion that verifies traces arrive at a Jaeger-compatible collector.
22	
23	- Smoke generates its own trace context (no OTel SDK dependency)
24	- Traceparent headers auto-injected into all network assertions when `otel` config is enabled
25	- `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger API to confirm trace reception
26	- Zero overhead when disabled
27	
28	## Configuration Schema
29	
30	### Global `otel` block
31	
32	```yaml
33	otel:
34	  enabled: true
35	  jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"   # Jaeger query API (not OTLP ingest)
36	  service_name: "cosmo-smoke"         # default: "smoke"
37	  trace_propagation: true             # auto-inject traceparent into network assertions
38	```
39	
40	### Per-test `otel_trace` assertion
41	
42	```yaml
43	tests:
44	  - name: trace arrived at collector
45	    run: curl http://localhost:8080/health
46	    expect:
47	      otel_trace:
48	        jaeger_url: "http://jaeger:16686"      # overrides global
49	        service_name: "my-service"             # overrides global
50	        min_spans: 1                           # at least N spans received (default: 1)
51	        timeout: 5s                            # wait for trace to appear
52	```
53	
54	## Trace Context Model
55	
56	### W3C Traceparent Format
57	
58	All propagation uses the W3C `traceparent` header: `00-{traceID}-{spanID}-{flags}`
59	
60	- `traceID`: 32 hex chars (16 bytes), generated once per suite run
61	- `spanID`: 16 hex chars (8 bytes), generated per test
62	- `flags`: `01` (sampled)
63	
64	No OTel SDK dependency. Pure string construction from crypto/rand. The `tracestate` header is intentionally omitted — cosmo-smoke doesn't need vendor-specific trace context.
65	
66	### Hierarchy
67	
68	```
69	Suite (traceID)
70	├── Test 1 (spanID-1, traceparent injected into HTTP/gRPC/WS requests)
71	├── Test 2 (spanID-2, ...)
72	└── Test N (spanID-N, ...)
73	```
74	
75	Each test is a child span under the suite's trace. The `otel_trace` assertion queries by the suite's `traceID`.
76	
77	### Injection Points
78	
79	| Assertion Type | Injection Target |
80	|---------------|-----------------|
81	| `http` | `traceparent` HTTP request header |
82	| `grpc_health` | `traceparent` gRPC metadata key |
83	| `websocket` | `traceparent` in WebSocket handshake headers |
84	
85	When `otel` config is absent or `enabled: false`, no injection occurs. Zero overhead.
86	
87	## Trace Verification
88	
89	### Jaeger API Query
90	
91	The `otel_trace` assertion queries Jaeger's trace API:
92	
93	```
94	GET {jaeger_url}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={service_name}
95	```
96	
97	### Assertion Logic
98	
99	1. Build the Jaeger API URL from `jaeger_url` + `traceID` + `service_name`
100	2. Poll with 500ms interval until `timeout` expires
101	3. Parse Jaeger JSON response (`data[].spans[]`)
102	4. Assert `len(spans) >= min_spans`
103	5. Return `AssertionResult` with pass/fail
104	
105	### Error Cases
106	
107	| Condition | Result |
108	|-----------|--------|
109	| Collector unreachable | Fail with network error |
110	| Timeout with no spans | Fail: "no spans found for trace {id} within {timeout}" |
111	| Invalid collector URL | Fail: validation error at config parse time |
112	
113	### Jaeger Response Parsing
114	
115	Expected JSON structure:
116	
117	```json
118	{
119	  "data": [
120	    {
121	      "traceID": "abc123...",
122	      "spans": [
123	        { "operationName": "...", "spanID": "..." }
124	      ]
125	    }
126	  ]
127	}
128	```
129	
130	Assertion checks `data[0].spans` length against `min_spans`.
131	
132	## Architecture
133	
134	### New Files
135	
136	| File | Purpose |
137	|------|---------|
138	| `internal/runner/trace.go` | `traceContext` struct, W3C traceparent generation, child span creation |
139	| `internal/runner/assertion_otel.go` | `CheckOTelTrace` function + Jaeger HTTP client |
140	| `internal/runner/trace_test.go` | Trace context generation unit tests |
141	| `internal/runner/assertion_otel_test.go` | Verification tests with net/http/httptest mock Jaeger |
142	
143	### Modified Files
144	
145	| File | Change |
146	|------|--------|
147	| `internal/schema/schema.go` | Add `OTelConfig` struct at top level + `OTelTraceCheck` field on `Expect` |
148	| `internal/schema/validate.go` | Validate otel config: jaeger_url format, service_name required when enabled |
149	| `internal/runner/runner.go` | Initialize `traceContext` in `Run()`, pass to assertion execution |
150	| `internal/runner/assertion_network.go` | Inject `traceparent` header into HTTP requests when trace context available |
151	| `internal/runner/assertion_grpc.go` | Inject `traceparent` into gRPC metadata when trace context available |
152	| `internal/runner/assertion_ws.go` | Inject `traceparent` into WebSocket handshake headers when trace context available |
153	| `cmd/run.go` | Add `--otel-collector` CLI flag (override `otel.jaeger_url`) and `--no-otel` flag (disable at runtime) |
154	
155	### Data Flow
156	
157	```
158	smoke run
159	  └── Runner.Run(opts)
160	       ├── Parse OTelConfig from SmokeConfig
161	       ├── If otel.enabled: init traceContext (generate traceID)
162	       ├── For each test:
163	       │    ├── Create child spanID
164	       │    ├── For HTTP/gRPC/WS assertions:
165	       │    │    └── Inject traceparent into request BEFORE sending
166	       │    │        (at request-construction time inside each Check* function)
167	       │    ├── Run test command
168	       │    ├── For otel_trace assertion:
169	       │    │    └── Poll GET {jaeger}/api/traces/{traceID}?service={svc}
170	       │    └── Collect AssertionResult
171	       └── Return SuiteResult
172	```
173	
174	### Impure Assertion Note
175	
176	All 26 existing assertions are pure functions — no I/O beyond test command output. `otel_trace` departs from this by polling an external HTTP API. This is intentional: trace verification is inherently an external system check (like `port_listening` or `redis_ping`). The function signature accepts an `*http.Client` parameter for testability — tests inject an `httptest` mock, production uses the default client.
177	
178	### No New Dependencies
179	
180	All stdlib: `crypto/rand`, `encoding/hex`, `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `fmt`. W3C traceparent is a simple string format.
181	
182	## Design Decisions
183	
184	| Decision | Rationale |
185	|----------|-----------|
186	| No OTel SDK | Keeps "minimal deps" principle. W3C traceparent is trivially constructed. cosmo-smoke is a test runner, not a telemetry pipeline. |
187	| Jaeger API only (v1) | Most widely deployed. Tempo is Jaeger-compatible. Can add backends later. |
188	| Suite-level traceID | Correlates all tests from one `smoke run` invocation. Matches how teams use traces. |
189	| Per-test spanID | Allows identifying which test generated which downstream span. |
190	| Polling for verification | Traces take time to propagate through collector pipeline. 500ms interval balances latency vs load. |
191	| `min_spans` threshold | Default 1 means "any trace arrived." Configurable for stricter checks. |
192	| Override per assertion | `otel_trace` allows per-test `jaeger_url` and `service_name` for multi-service scenarios. |
193	
194	## Edge Cases
195	
196	- **No `otel` config**: No trace context created, no injection, no overhead
197	- **`otel` enabled but no network assertions**: Trace context created but unused. `otel_trace` assertion still works standalone.
